Japanese: 私はある事件で、 被告席に立つことになった。
English: It was my turn to sit in the defendant's chair.

Japanese: そして‥‥その“敵”に ‥‥救われてしまった‥‥
English: And I was saved... by that person I called my "enemy"...

Japanese: ‥‥自分がユルせなかった。 だから私は、検事局を去った。
English: I couldn't forgive myself for all that had happened. So I left the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: “検事・御剣　怜侍は死を選ぶ” ‥‥と、メモを残して‥‥
English: And I left that note... "Prosecutor Miles Edgeworth chooses death"...

Japanese: ‥‥フン、当然ね。
English: Hmph, as well you should have.

Japanese: 勝利を断たれた検事など、 死んで当然！
English: A prosecutor who has shamed himself with defeat should crawl into a hole and die!

Japanese: ‥‥だが、そうではなかった。
English: ...But that was not what happened.

Japanese: 検事局をはなれて、 私はようやく気づいた。
English: After I left the Prosecutor's Office, I finally came to realize something.

Japanese: あの敗北の瞬間こそが‥‥ すべての始まりだったのだ、と。
English: And it was in that moment of clarity that everything began to change.

Japanese: ‥‥な、何をバカな‥‥
English: Wh-What foolish nonsense...

Japanese: われわれは、あらゆる手段を 使って、被告を攻撃する。
English: We prosecutors use anything we can to attack the defendant.

Japanese: しかし、そのたびに‥‥
English: But every time we did so...

Japanese: どんな絶望的な状況でも、 決してあきらめることなく‥‥
English: No matter how desperate the situation... instead of giving up like most people,

Japanese: 食らいついてくる男がいた。 悪夢のような信念を持って‥‥
English: that man would hold strong with his undying faith.

Japanese: そして、私はいつしか‥‥その男を 信頼し始めていたのだ。
English: And then, before I knew it... I began to trust in that man as well.

Japanese: 同日　午後　２時３５分 地方裁判所　第３法廷
English: March 23, 2:35 PM District Court Courtroom No. 3

Japanese: では、審理を再開します。 ‥‥準備はよろしいですね。
English: Court will now reconvene. I assume both sides are ready?

Japanese: は‥‥はあ‥‥
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor...

Japanese: う‥‥うム‥‥
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor...

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人はともかく‥‥
English: ...I can understand the defense acting like this,

Japanese: なぜ、あなたまで半死半生 なのですかッ！　御剣検事！
English: however, why do you also seem distraught, Mr. Edgeworth!?

Japanese: い、いやその。 ‥‥シ、シツレイした。
English: I... that is... It's nothing, Your Honor.

Japanese: （どうしたんだ？ 御剣のヤツ‥‥）
English: (What's wrong with Edgeworth...?)

Japanese: ‥‥想像を絶するような コトがあったみたいね。
English: It looks like something unexpected just happened to him.

Japanese: それでは、御剣検事。
English: Now then, Mr. Edgeworth. If you could please tell the court the results of the

Japanese: まずは、遺書の筆跡鑑定‥‥ 結果を報告してください。
English: handwriting analysis on Ms. Inpax's suicide note...

Japanese: う‥‥うム。 ザンネンながら‥‥
English: Y...Yes, Your Honor. Unfortunately...

Japanese: ‥‥これは‥‥、 ホンモノの遺書ではない。
English: We have discovered that this suicide note is a forgery.

Japanese: なんだって！
English: What!?

Japanese: ど、どういうことですかッ！
English: What do you mean, Mr. Edgeworth!?

Japanese: これは‥‥この遺書は‥‥ 偽造されたものだとわかった！
English: This... This note was not written by Ms. Inpax herself! It is a fake!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ 御剣検事！　いったい‥‥
English: Order! Order! Order! Mr. Edgeworth! Would you care to explain what is going on!?

Japanese: ダレの筆跡だったのですか！
English: If this was not written by Ms. Inpax, then who wrote it!?

Japanese: さらに詳しい検査が必要だが‥‥
English: We would need more time to do a more detailed analysis, however...

Japanese: どうやらこれは、被害者 藤見野　イサオの筆跡らしい。
English: It appears that the handwriting matches that of the victim, Mr. Juan Corrida.

Japanese: い‥‥イサオさん‥‥？
English: Mr. C-Corrida...?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥
English: Well, well...

Japanese: 由利恵さんの遺書には、 書かれていなかったようね。
English: It looks like Ms. Inpax never left a suicide note after all.

Japanese: ‥‥王都楼　真悟のこと‥‥
English: She never wrote anything about Engarde...

Japanese: ‥‥しかし！
English: However!

Japanese: たとえ、この遺書が ニセモノだったとしても‥‥
English: Your Honor. Even though this suicide note is indeed a fake,

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟はそれを 知らなかったにちがいない！
English: Mr. Engarde could not have known that, and so that facts remain unchanged!

Japanese: この遺書をホンモノだと思いこみ、 奪いかえそうとしたのだ！
English: Acting under the assumption that it was real, he had plotted to possess it!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ じゅうぶん、あり得ますな。
English: Hmm... That does sound very plausible.

Japanese: （“王都楼さんは、遺書が 偽造だと知らなかった‥‥”）
English: (This theory that Engarde had no idea that the suicide note was fake...)

Japanese: （この主張‥‥ちょっと ムジュンしてないか？）
English: (Something seems a little wrong with it...)

Japanese: ただ今の主張は、これまでの検事の 立証とムジュンしています！
English: The defense believes that the theory the prosecution has stated contradicts testimony!

Japanese: もし、この法廷で御剣検事が 立証してきたことが事実なら‥‥
English: If everything the prosecution has proven up to this point is true...

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟は、遺書がニセモノで あることを知っていたはずです！
English: Then it's impossible for Mr. Engarde to not have known it was a fake!

Japanese: い、いかがですか？　御剣検事！
English: Well, Mr. Edgeworth?? Your response?

Japanese: ‥‥この遺書がホンモノか どうか‥‥そんなことよりも、
English: I believe that even more important than finding out if this note is real or not,

Japanese: そこの男がムネにぶら下げている バッジがホンモノかどうか‥‥
English: is whether the attorney's badge pinned to that man's chest is real...

Japanese: そっちのほうが問題だと考える！
English: That is the million dollar question!

Japanese: ‥‥私も同じことを 考えていました。
English: Yes, I agree.

Japanese: 私もよ、なるほどくん。
English: Me too.

Japanese: （‥‥ハズレ、か）
English: (I'm going to take a wild guess and say that I messed up again...?)

Japanese: これは‥‥ なんでしたっけ？
English: What is this little item called again...?

Japanese: び、ビデオカメラですよ。 ‥‥超小型の。
English: Um, a video camera, Your Honor. Well, a very small one, but...

Japanese: あ、そうでした。 キカイに弱いもので‥‥
English: Oh, that's right. A camera. Ah, you kids and your fancy toys nowadays...

Japanese: 御剣検事！　キミはさっき、 こう主張していたはずだ！
English: Mr. Edgeworth! Earlier, you claimed that Mr. Engarde knew of the existence of this note

Japanese: “この遺書の存在を知ったのは、 被害者を監視していたからだ”
English: because he was spying on the victim. Isn't that right?

Japanese: それならば！
English: If that were true...

Japanese: 当然、被害者がそれを偽造 したことも、知っているはずだ！
English: Then this means Mr. Engarde would have known that the victim had forged the note!

Japanese: ぐわああッ！
English: Aaack!

Japanese: この遺書がニセモノだった。 それを被告人が知っていた。
English: So then, the defendant knew this suicide note was a fake.

Japanese: そうなると‥‥ とんでもないコトになります。
English: And if that's true, then the situation has suddenly changed in a very dramatic way.

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおりです！
English: Exactly, Your Honor!

Japanese: 検察側の主張してきた “殺人の動機”が‥‥
English: The prosecution's theory as to what Mr. Engarde's motive for murder was...

Japanese: 一瞬にして、消えてしまう！
English: It has suddenly disappeared into thin air!

Japanese: （ダメだ‥‥）
English: (It's no use...)

Japanese: （なんかオカシイ気がするけど、 ハッキリしない‥‥）
English: (Something feels wrong, but I can't put my finger on what it is...)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥じつは、少し 気になることがあるのですが‥‥
English: Hmm... Actually, there is something I would like to ask.

Japanese: 御剣検事。あなたはさきほど、 こう言ったはずです。
English: Mr. Edgeworth. You had stated something earlier to the effect of

Japanese: 被告は、藤見野　イサオ氏の 私生活を監視していた、と。
English: the defendant had spied on Mr. Corrida's private life...

Japanese: 遺書の存在も、監視の おかげで気づいたのですよね？
English: I believe this would mean that he would have known about the note as well...?
(Th-That's it!)

Japanese: それならば、当然‥‥
English: Yes, and so naturally...

Japanese: 王都楼さんは、遺書が偽造で あることも知っていたはずだ！
English: This means Mr. Engarde would have known that the note was a fake!
Uungh!

Japanese: ‥‥静粛に！　静粛に！ コラッ！　弁護人ッ！
English: Order! Order! See here, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ‥‥え。ぼくですか？
English: ...Um, yes Your Honor?

Japanese: 監視のコトは、 私が思いついたのですぞ！
English: I was the one who thought of the spying thing!

Japanese: それを横どりして、 イチバンいいところを‥‥！
English: Jumping in and stealing my thunder like that is simply... I can't even describe it!

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥スミマセン。
English: Ah, yes... Sorry...

Japanese: 御剣検事をヘコませれば、 マゴにジマンできたのに‥‥
English: I could've even bragged about embarrassing Mr. Edgeworth to my grandchild had you not...

Japanese: ペナルティを与えますッ！
English: For that, I assign you a penalty, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ぐわああッ！ （自分で気づけばよかった‥‥）
English: Whaaaaa!? (If only I had kept my mouth shut...)

Japanese: この遺書がニセモノだった。 それを被告人が知っていた。
English: So then, the defendant knew this suicide note was a fake.

Japanese: そうなると‥‥ とんでもないコトになります。
English: And if that's true, then the situation has suddenly changed in a very dramatic way.

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおりです！
English: Exactly, Your Honor!

Japanese: 検察側の主張してきた “殺人の動機”が‥‥
English: The prosecution's theory as to what Mr. Engarde's motive for murder was...

Japanese: 一瞬にして、消えてしまう！
English: It has suddenly disappeared into thin air!

Japanese: し、しかし‥‥！
English: But Your Honor!

Japanese: 王都楼も、２４時間 監視していたワケではない！
English: It's not as if Mr. Engarde monitored Mr. Corrida 24 hours a day!

Japanese: 被害者は、王都楼の知らない ところで遺書を偽造したのだ！
English: Perhaps the victim wrote the note in a place Mr. Engarde didn't know of!

Japanese: ‥‥キミの口グセを、 そのままお返ししよう！
English: Well, right back at you, Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: “被害者がどこで偽造をしたか” ‥‥証拠で示してもらおうか！
English: Why don't you show us some proof that the victim made the forgery at an unknown place!?

Japanese: ぐはああアッ！
English: Gnnngh!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ ‥‥御剣検事。
English: Order! Order! Order! ...Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: どうやら、今度はあなたが 自ら、墓穴を掘ったようですな。
English: It looks like this time, it is you who has dug his own grave.

Japanese: うむむむむむ‥‥やはり‥‥
English: Unnnngh... As I figured...
Huh? ...As you figured...?

Japanese: やはり‥‥こうなって しまうのか‥‥
English: As I figured... It came down to this after all...

Japanese: どういうコトですか？ ‥‥御剣検事。
English: Mr. Edgeworth, you are not making any sense...

Japanese: 筆跡鑑定の結果を聞いたとき、 こうなることは予想できた。
English: When I heard the results of the handwriting analysis, I thought this might happen.

Japanese: ‥‥問題は‥‥その次だ。
English: The question is... "What next?"

Japanese: その‥‥つぎ？
English: What next...??

Japanese: 証拠による動機の証明が できなくなった以上‥‥
English: If the prosecution can't prove Mr. Engarde's motive through the evidence,

Japanese: 検察側は、ベツの方面から 犯行を立証しなければならない。
English: then we must prove it from another angle.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥ そうなりますな。
English: Well, I agree with you there...

Japanese: 裁判長。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: 検察側は‥‥ここで、証人を 呼びたいと思うのだが‥‥
English: The prosecution... would like to call a witness to the stand at this time...

Japanese: お。ベツにかまいませんよ。
English: Oh. Well, that's fine.

Japanese: それがその‥‥ちょっと、 めずらしい証人で‥‥
English: However... this witness... This witness is a little... unusual...

Japanese: （御剣らしくないな。 口ごもったりして‥‥）
English: (Edgeworth stuttering...? This is not like him at all.)

Japanese: どんな証人ですか？
English: Unusual? Well, what sort of witness is this person, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: “虎狼死家に殺害を依頼した 人物はだれだったのか？”
English: This witness is one who is perfectly fit to answer once and for all the question of,

Japanese: ‥‥そのギモンに、 カンペキに答えられる証人だ。
English: "Who was it that hired Shelly de Killer to commit murder?"

Japanese: バ‥‥バカな！
English: That's impossible!! Who in the...!?

Japanese: そんなコトを証言できる 証人なんて、いるわけがない！
English: No such person exists who can answer that question with such certainty!

Japanese: た‥‥たしかに！ いったい、その証人とは‥‥
English: Y-Yes! Mr. Edgeworth! Who is this witness!?

Japanese: そ‥‥それが‥‥その。
English: It is... It's... um...

Japanese: だれですか！
English: Yes!? Go on! Who is it!?

Japanese: 本人だ。 ‥‥虎狼死家　左々右エ門。
English: The man himself... Mr. Shelly de Killer.

Japanese: ほお。コロシヤさん。
English: Oh, Mr. de Killer.

Japanese: なんですッてェェェェェェッ！ コロシヤ　サザエモンンンッ！
English: W-W-Waaaaait!! Shelly de Killer!?

Japanese: あの‥‥コロシヤ‥‥というのは ‥‥殺し屋、なんですよね？
English: Um, you mean... The killer? Err... I mean the assassin?

Japanese: そのとおり‥‥だ。
English: Yes... Your Honor.

Japanese: 証言台に立つ、と？
English: He's coming here? To the witness stand...?

Japanese: ま。そういうコトに なるのだろうか‥‥
English: Well, yes, in a manner of speaking...

Japanese: その。特例ということで、 許可していただきたい。
English: I recognize that this is a very unusual circumstance, so I ask for your permission.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥弁護人。
English: Hmm... Well, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: は、はい？
English: Y-Yes?

Japanese: いかがですか？
English: Is this alright with you?

Japanese: （いかがもナニも‥‥）
English: (Do I have a choice here...?)

Japanese: （時間をかせぐためには、 モンクを言ってる場合じゃない）
English: (I can't really do much else to drag this trial out...)

Japanese: あ。イイと思いますけど。
English: The defense has no objections, Your Honor.

Japanese: イイのでしょうか‥‥
English: I wonder if it really is alright to do this...?

Japanese: では、とにかく証人を 入廷させてみようではないか！
English: Very well then. The prosecution calls our witness to the stand!

Japanese: （御剣‥‥ ほとんどヤケクソだぞ‥‥）
English: (Edgeworth... Is there no other way left to us...?)

Japanese: では、証人。
English: Now then, witness.

Japanese: ‥‥ええと、名前と‥‥ その、職業を。
English: ...Um, your name... and your, uh... occupation, please.

Japanese: かしこまりました。
English: Very good, sir.

Japanese: 名前は虎狼死家　左々右エ門。 ‥‥殺し屋、やっております。
English: My name is Shelly de Killer, and I am a professional assassin.

Japanese: ちょちょ、ちょ、ちょ‥‥‥ちょ。
English: I... I say!!

Japanese: ちょっと待ちなさい！
English: Wh-What is going on here!?

Japanese: ‥‥なんだろうか？
English: Your Honor?

Japanese: なんだろうか、じゃありません。 なんですかこの無線機は。
English: How can you remain so calm? And what is the meaning of this two-way radio...?

Japanese: ‥‥じつは、ついさっき 私のもとへ届いたものだ。
English: Actually, Your Honor, it was delivered to me just now... And it came with a condition.

Japanese: 電波の逆探知をしないという 条件で、証言をする、と。
English: As long as we do not trace its source, Mr. de Killer will testify to this court.

Japanese: （電話で呼び出されたのは、 そのコトだったのか‥‥）
English: (So this must be what that urgent phone call he got earlier was about...)

Japanese: いやいやいや。ダメですよ、 これはさすがに。
English: Oh no, this will not do. I cannot allow this in my court.

Japanese: だいいち、コロシヤ本人か どうかも不明ではないですか！
English: First of all, we can't even be sure this is really Mr. de Killer himself!

Japanese: 証人。あなたが虎狼死家だという 証拠を提示していただきたい。
English: Witness. Please present some sort of proof that you are in fact Shelly de Killer.

Japanese: いたしかたありませんね。 ‥‥そのまま、お待ちください。
English: I understand. Please wait a second.

Japanese: ‥‥おなか‥‥へった‥‥
English: I'm... sooo... hungry...

Japanese: ままま、真宵ちゃんッ！
English: M-M-Maya!

Japanese: 真宵っ！
English: Maya!

Japanese: い‥‥いきなり どうしましたか！　弁護人！
English: A... A voice! Mr. Wright! Can you confirm anything from this!?

Japanese: い、異議なし！
English: The defense has no objections to this person!

Japanese: そのヒトを左々右エ門と認めます！
English: We are satisfied that this man is indeed Shelly de Killer!

Japanese: またまた、ややこしいコトに なってきましたな‥‥
English: It looks like we have run into yet another unexpected turn of events...

Japanese: しかたありません。 とにかく、審理をつづけましょう。
English: Well, it doesn't seem like we have too many choices under these circumstances, so...

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、証人。
English: ...Now then, witness.

Japanese: 最初に１つだけ、 カクニンしておきたい。
English: There is one thing I would like to confirm before we speak of anything else.

Japanese: なんでしょうか。
English: And what would that be?

Japanese: あなたは依頼を受けて、 藤見野　イサオを殺害したか。
English: At the request of a client, you killed Mr. Juan Corrida. Is this correct?

Japanese: おっしゃるとおりです。 私がやらせていただきました。
English: It is as you say. I did indeed kill Mr. Corrida.

Japanese: ぐぐゥ‥‥
English: *gulp*

Japanese: では、証言していただこう。 あなたに依頼した、人物の名前を！
English: Now that we have answered that, let's move on to the name of your client!

Japanese: ‥‥かしこまりました。
English: ...Very well.

Japanese: ‥‥あ‥‥悪夢です。 こ、こんなことが‥‥
English: This is all just a bad dream... Yes, that's it, a bad dream...

Japanese: （左々右エ門‥‥ いったい、どう出るつもりだ？）
English: (Shelly de Killer... What is he going to say...?)

Japanese: ～“依頼人”について～
English: -- About My Client --

Japanese: 最初に１つ、申し上げて おきたいことがございます。
English: There is something I must first state.

Japanese: 殺し屋という商売は、依頼人との 信頼関係が、最も大切なのです。
English: To an assassin, nothing is more important than the trust between a client and himself.

Japanese: だからこそ、私はこうして証言台に 立っているのでございます。
English: And that is the reason I am here today on this witness stand.

Japanese: ‥‥名前を申し上げる前に、 それをごリカイいただけますか。
English: It is my wish that you grasp this concept before I give the name of my client.

Japanese: ‥‥ずいぶん、 バカていねいなヒトですね。
English: Hmm, Mr. de Killer seems to be a very clever man.

Japanese: まるで、私たちを バカにしているようです。
English: I'd almost say he seems to be mocking us.

Japanese: たしかに、この証人は われわれの敵でしかない。
English: While he may appear to be our enemy, Your Honor,

Japanese: しかし、虎狼死家の コトバにウソはない。
English: Mr. de Killer is only stating the truth.

Japanese: 彼は、今、証言したことを ずっと守ってきている。
English: He is no hypocrite. He has always stood by this one belief.

Japanese: ‥‥“依頼人との信頼関係”？
English: You mean about this "trust between his clients and himself" thing?

Japanese: マッタク、私たちのリカイを 超えた世界ですな。
English: Hmm, it seems to be a level of trust beyond what people like me can comprehend.

Japanese: ‥‥では、弁護人。尋問を。
English: ...Well, Mr. Wright? Are you ready to cross- examine the witness?

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥これからの尋問‥‥ まったく、先が読めないわね。
English: There's no way to know what's coming next, so stay cool and collected, Phoenix.

Japanese: ～“依頼人”について～
English: -- About My Client --

Japanese: 最初に１つ、申し上げて おきたいことがございます。
English: There is something I must first state.

Japanese: 殺し屋という商売は、依頼人との 信頼関係が、最も大切なのです。
English: To an assassin, nothing is more important than the trust between a client and himself.

Japanese: だからこそ、私はこうして証言台に 立っているのでございます。
English: And that is the reason I am here today on this witness stand.

Japanese: （‥‥なんでもいいから、 さっさとしゃべってほしいな）
English: (I don't really care about all this extra fluff. Just tell us the name already!)

Japanese: しかたないわ。 相手に合わせるのも大切よ。
English: Patience. Try to calm down a little. It's important to try and understand his mindset.

Japanese: きちょうめんな証人みたいだから ‥‥言いたいことを引き出すの。
English: He seems very steadfast and closed so you're going to have to work to get him to talk.

Japanese: （ぼくは人生相談の センセイじゃないぞ‥‥）
English: (I'm not his therapist, you know...)

Japanese: ‥‥名前を申し上げる前に、 それをごリカイいただけますか。
English: It is my wish that you grasp this concept before I give the name of my client.

Japanese: では、そろそろ‥‥依頼人の名前を 証言させていただきましょうか。
English: Now then, I do believe it's about time I revealed the name of my client, don't you agree?

Japanese: それはあとまわしにして、 さっさと依頼人の名前を‥‥
English: We can hear anything you have to say later. Can you please just tell us your client's...?

Japanese: 弁護士さん。あなたは ご自分の立場がわかっていない。
English: I don't think you understand your place, Mr. Attorney.

Japanese: 私は“最初に”申し上げたい ‥‥こう言っているのです。
English: I said this is something I must first state. Do you know what the word "first" means?

Japanese: ど‥‥どうぞ。
English: S-Sorry... Go on...

Japanese: どうもみなさん、この証人には コシが引けているように見えます。
English: Well, it appears this is one witness you can't badger, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （裁判長は知らないんだっけ ‥‥真宵ちゃんのコト）
English: (That's only because you don't know about Maya's situation...)

Japanese: “信頼関係”‥‥？
English: The trust between you and your client...?

Japanese: 私どもは、正確にシゴトを させていただきます。
English: I provide my services in a fast and efficient manner.

Japanese: そのかわり、お客さまも、 決して私どものことを口外しない。
English: In exchange, I trust that my clients are discreet about me and my identity.

Japanese: 商売がら、あまりヒトさまに 知られてはコマりますからね。
English: If too many people knew my face, it would be quite troublesome.

Japanese: ‥‥そのワリに、こうして堂々と 証言台に立っていますが？
English: And that is why you're testifying in this manner?

Japanese: 依頼人に殺人容疑がかかったのは はじめてでございますから。
English: This is the first time one of my clients has ever been accused of murder.

Japanese: 虎狼死家の名にかけて、 なんとかせねば、と思いまして。
English: I must preserve the De Killer name so my clients can trust me.

Japanese: その“信頼関係”がウラ切られる コトはないんですか？
English: But couldn't someone stab you in the back and break your trust?

Japanese: 今までは、ありませんでした。 もし、そんなことがあれば‥‥
English: It has never happened before, but if it ever did...

Japanese: あ、あれば‥‥？
English: Y-Yes...?

Japanese: 依頼人といえども ヨウシャはいたしません。
English: That person wouldn't be my client for very long.

Japanese: かならずや‥‥
English: They would certainly...

Japanese: も、もうケッコウです！
English: Th-That's enough! Please, no more!

Japanese: ‥‥かしこまりました。 それはともかく。
English: Very well. It was only a hypothetical anyway.

Japanese: “信頼関係”‥‥？
English: The trust between you and your client...?

Japanese: 私どもは、正確にシゴトを させていただきます。
English: I provide my services in a fast and efficient manner.

Japanese: そのかわり、お客さまも、 決して私どものことを口外しない。
English: In exchange, I trust that my clients are discreet about me and my identity.

Japanese: それが、殺し屋と依頼人の 仁義というものです。
English: These are the roles and duties an assassin and his client are to carry out.

Japanese: ところで‥‥証人はさっき、 こう証言しましたね。
English: I'm sorry, but I was wondering about something you just said.

Japanese: “今回の依頼人は、仁義にはずれた コトをした”‥‥と。
English: You said that your client had already "broken the rules"...

Japanese: 自分の罪をヒトに着せる‥‥ 人間として、サイテイです。
English: A person who frames another is the worst kind of human.

Japanese: ‥‥だから、あなたのほうから ウラ切るんですか？　信頼関係を。
English: And that's why you feel you can betray this person?

Japanese: “仁義”をリカイできない依頼人 に、信頼関係などありません。
English: I have no trust relation with a client who can't understand their assigned role.

Japanese: （‥‥リクツっぽい 殺し屋だな、まったく‥‥）
English: (Just my luck... An assassin with a conscience. Who would've figured...?)

Japanese: ‥‥いかがですか？　みなさん。 私の考え、リカイされましたか？
English: Now then, everyone. Do you think you can understand my logic?

Japanese: （リカイできるか！）
English: (This case just keeps getting better and better!)

Japanese: できなければ、このまま 先へ進めませんが‥‥
English: If you can't, then I'm afraid we can't proceed...

Japanese: ‥‥とてもよく リカイできたと思います。
English: Everyone understands your point, I think. Really.

Japanese: それならば‥‥
English: In that case...

Japanese: そろそろ、私の方も ココロの準備ができてきました。
English: I believe I am prepared to disclose the information you seek.

Japanese: 証人にとって“信頼関係”が 大切なのは、了解した。
English: You have made it crystal clear that you value trust over all else. I believe we are ready.

Japanese: ‥‥さよう。
English: ...Excellent.

Japanese: ‥‥それは少し、おかしいですね。
English: That seems a little strange to me...

Japanese: 証人はこれから、依頼人の名前を 証言しようとしています。
English: I mean, you're about to tell us the name of your client.

Japanese: 依頼人にとっては、ものすごく メイワクだと思いますが。
English: I would think that this would be very bad for them.

Japanese: ‥‥いたしかたありません。
English: It doesn't matter to me.

Japanese: このたびの依頼人は、仁義に はずれたコトをしておりますから。
English: This client has already broken the rules and acted outside of their prescribed role.

Japanese: じじ、じんぎ‥‥？
English: Their role...?

Japanese: 自分が助かるために、 他人に罪をなすりつける‥‥
English: This person tried to implicate another of the crime in order to save themselves.

Japanese: これは、とてもユルされるべき コトではありません。
English: And this is a trespass that cannot be forgiven.

Japanese: 殺し屋が、ナニをエラそうに‥‥！
English: You... Who gave you the right to be so high and mighty...!?

Japanese: 今、発言した方‥‥ シツレイですが、死にたいですか？
English: To the gentleman who spoke just now... Excuse me, but would you care to die?

Japanese: い、いえッ！　そんなッ！ なんでもないですッ！
English: Ah, no! No! I, uh, didn't say anything!

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ‥‥）
English: (The judge had better watch himself...)

Japanese: リカイしましたから、 依頼人の名前を教えてください！
English: We understand, so please tell us the name of your client!!

Japanese: ‥‥そうはまいりません。
English: I'm afraid I cannot do that.

Japanese: まだ、私の方には 言うべきコトが残っています。
English: I still have a few things to say before I do.

Japanese: （くそ‥‥！ もったいぶりやがって‥‥）
English: (Aaargh! That egomaniacal...)

Japanese: イライラするのは カラダによくありません。
English: It's not good for your health to be so aggravated.

Japanese: ‥‥長生きできませんよ。
English: You won't live very long if you let everything bother you...

Japanese: （殺し屋に言われると ‥‥リアルすぎて、コワい）
English: (Somehow, that coming from an assassin makes it less than comforting...)

Japanese: どうしましたか？
English: What is it?

Japanese: （うう‥‥依頼人の名前を聞く 決心がつかない‥‥）
English: (Umm... Now I can't bring myself to ask the client's name...)

Japanese: ‥‥では、かわりに私が聞こう。
English: If you can't ask it, Mr. Wright, then I will.

Japanese: 証人！
English: Witness!

Japanese: 藤見野　イサオの殺害を依頼した 人物の名前を証言してもらいたい！
English: What is the name of your client who requested the murder of Mr. Juan Corrida!?

Japanese: かしこまりました。 そのヒトのお名前は‥‥
English: That person's name is...

Japanese: ‥‥カミヤ　キリオ‥‥。
English: ...Adrian Andrews...

Japanese: ええええええ エエエエエエエエエェェ< > ェェッ
English: ええええええええええ<Edgeworth> ええ<Judge> Whaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaat< >

Japanese: しょ‥‥証人ッ！
English: W-Witness!!

Japanese: さっきキサマが言った 名前と、ちがうではないかッ！
English: That's not who you told me it was earlier!

Japanese: ナニをおっしゃいます、 検事さん‥‥
English: Pray tell, what are you talking about, Mr. Prosecutor?

Japanese: 依頼人は、カミヤ　キリオさま‥‥ まちがいございません。
English: I should think I know my own client, and it is Adrian Andrews.

Japanese: なんだとおおおオオオオッ！
English: Whaaaaaaaat!?!?

Japanese: ば‥‥バカな‥‥！ さっきの電話では‥‥
English: Th-This can't be! On the phone earlier...

Japanese: い、いったい‥‥ どうなってるんでしょう？
English: Wh-What's going on here...?

Japanese: コロシヤが、ウラ切った ようね‥‥御剣検事を。
English: My guess is that Mr. de Killer just stabbed Mr. Edgeworth in the back.

Japanese: 御剣を‥‥？
English: Stabbed Edgeworth in the back...?

Japanese: たぶん、開廷前の打ち合わせでは、 ちがう名前を証言するハズだった。
English: I'm sure in order to get an audience with this court,

Japanese: “王都楼　真悟”‥‥と。
English: Mr. de Killer told him a different name... "Matt Engarde", perhaps...?

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥）
English: (I knew it...)

Japanese: こ‥‥これは、ワナだッ！
English: This... This is outrageous! I was deceived!

Japanese: この証人は‥‥大ウソを ついている！
English: This witness is telling a very serious lie!

Japanese: し、しかし‥‥この証人を 召喚したのは、あなた自身ですぞ！
English: B-But you were the one who summoned this witness!

Japanese: ぐ‥‥ッ！
English: Grk!

Japanese: は‥‥はかったな‥‥ 虎狼死家　左々右エ門‥‥！
English: Grr... Y-You... Shelly de Killer...!

Japanese: ‥‥私の証言に ウソはございません。
English: My testimony is the truth.

Japanese: 今、裁判にかけられている被告 ‥‥王都楼さま、でしたか。
English: The defendant at the moment is Matt Engarde, am I correct?

Japanese: ただちに、彼を無罪にして いただきたいと存じます。
English: All I wish to do is help procure his acquittal.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: なんだか‥‥
English: Wow...

Japanese: いきなり、勝っちゃいそうな フンイキね。
English: All of a sudden, it feels like we can actually win this.

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: Yeah...

Japanese: ‥‥検察側は、被告の動機を 立証できませんでした。
English: The prosecution has failed to provide a motive and has instead,

Japanese: この遺書が、被害者による 偽造であること‥‥そして、
English: provided this suicide note, which is a forgery created by the victim.

Japanese: そのことを、被告は知っていた 可能性があったからです。
English: Furthermore, there is a possibility the defendant himself knew it was a fake.

Japanese: さらに‥‥
English: But most definitive of all,

Japanese: 今、殺し屋自身の証言で ハッキリしました。
English: we have heard from the assassin himself; the name of his client.

Japanese: 彼に殺害を依頼したのは ‥‥被告人ではなかった！
English: Mr. de Killer's client who requested the murder was not the defendant at all!

Japanese: ‥‥バカな‥‥
そうなれば、当然‥‥
English: With all this evidence, it is obvious to me that this means that Mr. Matt Engarde...

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟は 《無罪》ということになります！
English: Is innocent!

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、やはり コンランされているようですね。
English: I seem to have caused you all a bit of confusion.

Japanese: しばらく交信を切ります。 ‥‥ご相談ください。
English: Please, continue your discussion, and call me when you have reached a verdict.

Japanese: ‥‥係官！　大至急、 華宮　霧緒を連れてきなさい！
English: Bailiff! Please bring Ms. Adrian Andrews in immediately!

Japanese: どうするの？　今なら、 無罪判決をもらえるわ。
English: What now? With the way this is going, Engarde will be found innocent.

Japanese: 真宵を助けるなら‥‥もう、 最後のチャンスかもしれない。
English: This may be our last chance... to save Maya...

Japanese: え、ええ‥‥でも‥‥
English: Yeah... But...

Japanese: 御剣はまちがっていない。 虎狼死家はウソをついています！
English: But Edgeworth is right. De Killer is lying!

Japanese: 王都楼は‥‥ぼくの依頼人は‥‥ 有罪なんですよ！
English: And Engarde... my client... I know he's guilty!

Japanese: （このまま‥‥ぼくは、 勝ってしまっていいのか！）
English: (Can I live with myself if I win this!?)

Japanese: まさか‥‥検察側の証人が、 被告の無実を立証しようとは！
English: Who would've believed that the prosecution's own witness would absolve the defendant!?

Japanese: 裁判長！　検察側は、 詳しい尋問を要求する！
English: Your Honor! The prosecution requests permission to further question the witness!

Japanese: 虎狼死家　左々右エ門は、 ウソをついているのだ！
English: Shelly de Killer is certainly lying under oath!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 私じゃないわ！
English: It wasn't me!

Japanese: みなさん、聞いてください！ これは‥‥みんな、ウソです！
English: Listen! Everyone! Please! That testimony just now... It was all one big lie!

Japanese: き、霧緒さん‥‥
English: Ms. Andrews...

Japanese: 遺書は‥‥ニセモノだった かもしれません。
English: The suicide note may have been a fake.

Japanese: でも‥‥！
English: But!

Japanese: あの男は‥‥王都楼は、 由利恵さんを死に追いやった！
English: That man... Matt... He's the reason Celeste died!

Japanese: そして、藤見野の死も‥‥ あいつが仕組んだことなんです！
English: And Juan's death... It was all because he got pulled into Matt's twisted world!

Japanese: ウソです‥‥ こんな‥‥ヒドい話‥‥
English: That testimony just now... You have to believe me... It was a horrible, horrible lie...

Japanese: しかし‥‥コロシヤ自身が 証言しているのですぞ。
English: But... Mr. de Killer himself has testified...

Japanese: 依頼人はあなたである、と。
English: He has named you as his client.
No! That's not true!

Japanese: それに、あなたに関する 証拠なら、いくらでもある。
English: Also, there is quite a bit of evidence that points to you.

Japanese: ナイフにボタン、トノサマンの 着ぐるみ‥‥
English: The knife and button, donning the Nickel Samurai's costume...

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥
English: But that's... That's...

Japanese: ‥‥動機もあります。
English: You even have a motive.

Japanese: 天野　由利恵さんは、 あなたの中で、大きな存在でした。
English: We know that Ms. Celeste Inpax was a large part of your life.

Japanese: ‥‥彼女を死に追いやった、 男たちへの復讐‥‥
English: You wanted to follow her... And you wanted revenge against the two who hurt her.

Japanese: 殺意を抱く理由として、 じゅうぶんだと言えます。
English: I would say you have plenty of reasons to want them both dead.

Japanese: そ‥‥そんな‥‥
成歩堂さん！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: あなたは‥‥あなただって、 知ってるんでしょう！
English: You... You know the truth! Tell them!

Japanese: ホントのことを！ ‥‥真犯人が、だれなのか‥‥
English: Tell them the real story... Who the real killer is... Tell them!

Japanese: ‥‥おねがいします‥‥ ‥‥助けて‥‥
English: Please... Help me...

Japanese: （‥‥たしかに‥‥ ぼくは、知っている！）
English: (Yes, I know the truth...)

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor?

Japanese: 審理はここにいたって、 １つの結論にたどりつきました。
English: I believe we have reached the end of this trial.

Japanese: ‥‥弁護側の意見を 述べていただきたい。
English: Therefore, I ask the defense for any final words or opinions.

Japanese: （決めなければならない‥‥）
English: (I have to decide...)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんを助けて 無罪判決を取るか‥‥）
English: (Do I take the not guilty verdict and save Maya...)

Japanese: （そのチャンスを捨てて、 犯人の遺留品を待つか‥‥？）
English: (Or do I throw this chance away and wait for Gumshoe's new evidence...?)

Japanese: （‥‥どうする！）
English: (What am I supposed to do!?)

Japanese: 裁判長！‥‥弁護側は要求します。
English: Your Honor! The defense will make a request...

Japanese: （ダメだ‥‥ コトバが出てこない‥‥！）
English: (It's no use... I can't... It feels like I've lost my voice...!)

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥
English: Phoenix...

Japanese: ‥‥ダメです、千尋さん。 無罪判決は受け取れません。
English: I can't do it, Mia... I can't accept a not guilty.

Japanese: あなたは‥‥弁護士なのよ。
English: You are a lawyer.

Japanese: わかっています。
English: I know.

Japanese: でも‥‥王都楼　真悟は 殺人者なんです。
English: But... But Matt Engarde is a killer; a murderer!

Japanese: その罪は‥‥つぐなわなければ ならない。
English: I can't... I can't let him get away with this. I can't let someone else take the fall.

Japanese: 霧緒さんに、それを 負わせてしまったら‥‥
English: If I let Ms. Andrews be convicted,

Japanese: ぼく自身が、王都楼と 変わらないことになってしまう。
English: then I am no better than Engarde.

Japanese: ‥‥それに‥‥ 認めたくないけど、
English: And even though I don't want to admit it, I have to face the fact

Japanese: ぼくが真相を知ることが できたのは、あいつのおかげです。
English: that it is because of Edgeworth that I now know the real truth.

Japanese: あいつには、何度も有罪判決を 勝ちとるチャンスがあった。
English: He could've gotten Engarde convicted so many times over,

Japanese: ‥‥でも、そうしなかった。
English: but he never took a single one of those chances.

Japanese: 今、ここでぼくが 判決を受けとってしまったら‥‥
English: If I take this verdict right now...

Japanese: あいつの信頼を ウラ切ることになります。
English: I'd be betraying his trust.
(...His trust...?)

Japanese: （今まで‥‥ 考えたこともなかった）
English: (I never thought about it until now...)

Japanese: （ぼくは‥‥あいつを “信頼”しているのか‥‥？）
English: (I... I trust him...?)

Japanese: ‥‥そうね。
English: Yes, you do.

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂くん。 おねがいします。
English: Mr. Wright. Your opinion, please.

Japanese: 弁護側は‥‥虎狼死家に対する 尋問を要求します。
English: The defense requests that we be allowed to further question Mr. de Killer.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: A-Am I hearing you correctly, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂‥‥
English: Wright...

Japanese: し‥‥しかし！
English: But... But...

Japanese: あの証人は、被告人の無実を 証言しているのですぞ！
English: That witness has cleared your client through his testimony! Your job here is done!

Japanese: 証人のウソを見抜いて、 真実を見つける‥‥
English: I'm not done yet. To see through this witness' lies and find the truth...

Japanese: それが、ぼくのシゴトです！
English: THAT is my job, Your Honor!

Japanese: （まだ‥‥犯人の遺留品が 残っている‥‥）
English: (There's still more evidence to look at...)

Japanese: （この法廷に そいつがとどいたとき‥‥）
English: (And I'm sure that once those pieces arrive here in this very courtroom...)

Japanese: （ぼくはかならず奇跡を起こす！）
English: (a miracle will occur!)

Japanese: わかりました。 ‥‥審理をつづけましょう。
English: Very well. The trial will continue.

Japanese: 御剣検事。 ‥‥交信を再開してください。
English: Mr. Edgeworth. Please re-establish connection with Mr. de Killer.

Japanese: 了解した！
English: Right away, Your Honor!

Japanese: ‥‥判決は下されましたかな？
English: ...Has a verdict been reached?

Japanese: ‥‥その前に。 もう少しお話をうかがいたい。
English: Before that, we would like to talk with you a little more.

Japanese: どういうことでしょう‥‥？
English: About?

Japanese: 依頼人の名前以外に、 何が必要なのですか？
English: All you needed from me was the name of my client. What else could you need me for?

Japanese: ま、まあその。
English: Well...

Japanese: ‥‥いちおう、証人が知っている ことは、ゼンブ聞いておきたい。
English: Actually, we would like to hear everything you know about this case.

Japanese: それがその‥‥決まりなのだ。
English: That is how things are... usually done.

Japanese: （なんだよ、“決まり”って‥‥）
English: (What is he talking about? "Usually done"...??)

Japanese: しかし‥‥いったい、 何を証言してもらうのですか？
English: But... What shall we have him testify about now?

Japanese: ‥‥では、依頼人について、 くわしく証言をおねがいする。
English: Mr. de Killer. If you don't mind, please testify about your client in more detail.

Japanese: ‥‥やれやれ。法廷というのも、 なかなかメンドウでございますね。
English: You legal people and your procedures. Is it any wonder no one likes to go to court?

Japanese: ～“依頼人”について・２～
English: -- About My Client, Pt. 2 --

Japanese: 何度も言いますが、依頼人は カミヤ　キリオさまでございます。
English: As I have already stated quite a few times, Adrian Andrews is my client. However.

Japanese: 私がユルせないのは、現場に 細工なさったことなのです。
English: One thing I simply cannot overlook is tampering with the scene of the crime.

Japanese: ご自分の罪を、他人に なすりつけようとなさるとは。
English: My client did it to frame another for the crime.

Japanese: なんでも、発見者のフリをして 現場に訪れたそうですが‥‥
English: While pretending to be the first person to discover the body and enter the scene,

Japanese: カミヤさまは最初から知っていた！ 藤見野さまが死んでいることを。
English: Adrian Andrews already knew from the very beginning that Juan Corrida was dead!

Japanese: その上で、ナイフやボタンなどの 証拠をでっち上げて‥‥
English: But even more appalling is the creation and planting of the "knife" and "button".

Japanese: それは、仁義にはずれた行為と 言わねばなりません。
English: That act is what I was referring to when I said my client had "broken the rules".

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: ワレワレは、とんでもない ゴカイをしていたようですね。
English: This is a most unexpected turn of events... For the... um, fifth time now...?

Japanese: これで、すべてが ハッキリしたようです。
English: However, this time, everything has finally been revealed.

Japanese: ま、待っていただきたい！
English: Just a second, Your Honor!

Japanese: ‥‥なんですか、御剣検事？
English: Yes, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: まだ‥‥尋問が残っている。
English: We still have the cross- examination to do...

Japanese: ‥‥しかし、この証言に 尋問は必要ないでしょう？
English: But you don't need to question testimony like this...

Japanese: そうですね？　弁護人。
English: Do you, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: いえ。しますよ、尋問。 （するしかないんだよな、これが）
English: Your Honor, the defense will question the witness. (As if I have a choice here!)

Japanese: え！　なぜ！
English: Huh!? Why!?

Japanese: この証人は、弁護側にとって 有利な証言をしているんですよ？
English: What this witness has said is nothing but beneficial to the defense's case!

Japanese: その証言に尋問する、 ということは‥‥
English: If you scrutinize the testimony, then...

Japanese: （“有利な証言”のウソを暴く コトになるんだよな‥‥）
English: (...Then I'll expose the lies in that oh-so-"beneficial" testimony, I suppose.)

Japanese: ‥‥もう、ワケがわかりません。
English: I don't understand what's going on anymore...

Japanese: （ぼくもだ‥‥）
English: (That makes two of us...)

Japanese: ～“依頼人”について・２～
English: -- About My Client, Pt. 2 --

Japanese: 何度も言いますが、依頼人は カミヤ　キリオさまでございます。
English: As I have already stated quite a few times, Adrian Andrews is my client. However.

Japanese: 私がユルせないのは、現場に 細工なさったことなのです。
English: One thing I simply cannot overlook is tampering with the scene of the crime.

Japanese: ご自分の罪を、他人に なすりつけようとなさるとは。
English: My client did it to frame another for the crime.

Japanese: なんでも、発見者のフリをして 現場に訪れたそうですが‥‥
English: While pretending to be the first person to discover the body and enter the scene,

Japanese: カミヤさまは最初から知っていた！ 藤見野さまが死んでいることを。
English: Adrian Andrews already knew from the very beginning that Juan Corrida was dead!

Japanese: その上で、ナイフやボタンなどの 証拠をでっち上げて‥‥
English: But even more appalling is the creation and planting of the "knife" and "button".

Japanese: それは、仁義にはずれた行為と 言わねばなりません。
English: That act is what I was referring to when I said my client had "broken the rules".

Japanese: どうするつもり？ なるほどくん。
English: What are you going to do now, Phoenix?

Japanese: ‥‥暴くしかありませんよね。 証言のウソを‥‥
English: All I can do now is expose the lies.

Japanese: そうだけど‥‥当然、 不利になるわ。王都楼　真悟に。
English: That's true... However, you realize that will be very bad for our client, right?

Japanese: （うう‥‥なんだか コンランしてきたけど‥‥）
English: (Nnngh... I'm so confused...)

Japanese: （とにかく！　審理を終わらせちゃ いけないんだ‥‥！）
English: (But the one thing I know for sure is I can't let this trial end yet!)

Japanese: どうしました？　弁護人。
English: What is it, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: （‥‥ヘタにゆさぶると、 虎狼死家にあやしまれるな）
English: (If I press him the wrong way, it might raise suspicions on his end...)

Japanese: （時間かせぎがバレた時点で ナニをするかわからない‥‥）
English: (But I have to do something to waste more time...)

Japanese: あの‥‥証人。 依頼のことですけど。
English: Um... Witness. About requesting a hit...

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes?

Japanese: いくらぐらいなんですか？ ‥‥依頼料は。
English: How much is your fee...?

Japanese: ‥‥弁護士さんも おヒトが悪い‥‥
English: I see you are also quite a dark-hearted man, Mr. Attorney...

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: シゴトのハナシでしたら、 法廷が終わってから‥‥
English: If you would like to talk business, we can do so after the trial...

Japanese: い、いやいや！ ぼくが依頼をしたいワケではな
English: Ack! NONONO! I'm not thinking of hiring--

Japanese: ななな、成歩堂くんッ！
English: M-M-Mr. Wright!!

Japanese: ははは、はいッ！
English: Y-Yes!?

Japanese: あ、あ、あなたは‥‥
English: Y-Y-You...

Japanese: コロす気なんですね！ ‥‥この、私をッ！
English: You want to kill me... You want me dead... DON'T YOU!?!?

Japanese: ええええッ！ なんでぼくが、裁判長を
English: Whaaaat!? Why would you think something like that, Your Hon--

Japanese: ユーザイですッ！ 成歩堂　龍一！　ユーザイ！
English: Guilty! MR. PHOENIX WRIGHT! You are hereby declared GUILTY!!

Japanese: ‥‥証人。 つづけていただこう。
English: ...Witness. Let's continue...

Japanese: なぜ、依頼人の名前を バクロしたのか‥‥
English: Why did you disclose the name of your client? They are your client, are they not?

Japanese: 殺人をヒトに依頼する時点で ユルせないと思いますけど‥‥
English: I would think that most people wouldn't be able to overlook a person hiring another to kill.

Japanese: それを言われては、私どもの 商売は成り立ちません。
English: If I had a problem with such a thing, I wouldn't be very effective at my job.

Japanese: はあ‥‥ （転職すればいいのに）
English: Ah... Yeah... (Well, a change in occupation might be good for you...)

Japanese: ‥‥しかし、 これだけは言えます。
English: However, I will say this.

Japanese: 私どもが手を下したとしても‥‥ 依頼人は、やはり罪人なのです。
English: Even though I am the one that does the deed, my clients are always the real guilty party.

Japanese: それはモチロン、そうだろう。
English: That goes without saying, Mr. de Killer.

Japanese: その罪の重さを背負って 生きていくのが宿命なのです。
English: And their fate is to live with the knowledge of their guilt on their shoulders.

Japanese: それなのに‥‥今回の依頼人は、 そのセキニンを逃れようとした。
English: However, my client this time thought that they could run away from their guilt...

Japanese: あの、ナイフやボタンの コトですか‥‥？
English: Are you talking about the button and the knife?

Japanese: それに、 名刺のコトもございます。
English: Yes, and my business card.

Japanese: （コイツのことか‥‥）
English: (Oh, this card...)

Japanese: 警察のみなさんの手間を はぶくため‥‥、
English: So that no one has to waste their time, including the police...

Japanese: 私どもはいつも、たゆまぬ 努力をしておるのです。
English: I always make it a point to make things as easy as possible.

Japanese: ドリョク‥‥
English: You try to make things easy...?

Japanese: 名刺はもちろんのこと、 手口もわかりやすく工夫を。
English: My business card makes it very easy to identify who carried out the "service".

Japanese: （企業努力、ってヤツか‥‥？）
English: (He's pretty devoted to his work...)

Japanese: それを‥‥、あろうことか、 ホトケさまにナイフをつきたて、
English: But to disregard everything... to go and stab the deceased with a knife,

Japanese: 私の名刺まで かくしてしまうとは‥‥
English: and even hide my card from sight...

Japanese: とうてい、ユルせませんな。
English: That is something I cannot overlook.

Japanese: （本人が目の前にいないと、 本気か冗談かわからないな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... It's really hard to tell if he's being truthful or not without him being here...)

Japanese: ふつう依頼人は、 そういうコトはしないんですか？
English: So you're saying most clients wouldn't do such a thing?

Japanese: そうでございますね。
English: That is correct.

Japanese: みなさん、キホン的には アリバイ作りをなさるようです。
English: Usually, most people try to create an alibi for themselves.

Japanese: 弁護士さんも、ご用命の際は そのへんに気をつけて‥‥
English: If you should use my services, Mr. Attorney, I would suggest you plan for your alibi too...

Japanese: い、いやいや。だから、 依頼するつもりはないですよ！
English: Ah, no! I already told you! I have no intention of ever using your services!

Japanese: （なんでウタガイのマナコで 見られなきゃならんのだ！）
English: (Why does he keep looking at me like I'm the one on trial here!?)

Japanese: 最初から‥‥？
English: From the very beginning?

Japanese: そのとおり。 部屋を訪れる前からです。
English: That is correct. From before my client visited the room.

Japanese: なにしろ、依頼人なのですから。 知っていなければオカシイ。
English: All of my clients know precisely what the situation is at all times.

Japanese: （ホントにそうなのか‥‥？）
English: (I wonder if that's really true...)

Japanese: 妙だな‥‥
English: That's odd...

Japanese: ‥‥どうして そんなコトをしたのでしょう？
English: So why do you think your client did that?

Japanese: “どうして”とは？
English: What do you mean by "why"?

Japanese: 現場に細工する‥‥ とてもキケンなシゴトです。
English: Well, fiddling around at the scene of the crime is pretty risky.

Japanese: 殺害を依頼しても、自分が 現場に行っては、イミがない。
English: And why would someone who has requested a murder go to the crime scene anyway?

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ そりゃそうですねえ。
English: Hmm, that is true.

Japanese: ‥‥それだけ、王都楼さまに 罪を着せたかったのでしょう。
English: I assume it was probably done to frame Mr. Engarde.

Japanese: それならば、ついでに あなたに依頼すれば‥‥
English: If that's the case, then why didn't the person just request that you do it...?

Japanese: ザンネンですが。 それはムリというものです。
English: Sadly, that is not possible.

Japanese: え？
English: Huh?

Japanese: 私どものシゴトは“殺害”まで。 そして、かならず名刺を残します。
English: My job is to kill. That is all. And to leave my business card behind, naturally.

Japanese: それが、ヒトさまにメイワクを かけない、私どもの仁義なのです。
English: The business card is so my clients may escape blame. To protect them is my duty.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 復讐のためとはいえ、ご自分の罪を ヒトさまに着せる‥‥
English: Even if they say it's for revenge, setting someone else up to take your fall...

Japanese: ‥‥それで 証言をしたわけですか。
English: And that's all you have to testify?

Japanese: はい。二度と こんなことがないように‥‥
English: Yes. And I pray that I will never be called to the stand again...

Japanese: “二度と”‥‥ということは、 つづけるんですか？　これからも。
English: "Again"...? As in you plan to continue?

Japanese: ‥‥なにぶん、４代目が 見つかりませんのでねえ。
English: I must, as I have yet to find a person to take my place, and become the fourth successor.

Japanese: あ。弁護士さん、いかがですか？ 第二の人生に‥‥
English: Actually, how would you like a new life, Mr. Attorney...?

Japanese: え。
English: Excuse me?

Japanese: い、いえいえ！ ぼくはケッコウです！
English: Ah, no no no! I'm fine! Really!

Japanese: ‥‥どうだか。
English: Are you really now...?

Japanese: （裁判長‥‥ぼくのコト、 どう考えているんだ‥‥？）
English: (I wonder what kind of man the judge thinks I am now...)

Japanese: ‥‥証言は、セイカクに おねがいしたいですね。
English: Thank you so much for taking the time to testify, Mr. de Killer.

Japanese: どういうことですか？
English: What is the meaning of that attitude?

Japanese: 霧緒さんは、現場の控え室に 入ったとき‥‥
English: When Adrian Andrews entered the victim's room,

Japanese: イサオさんが殺害されて いることは、知らなかった！
English: your "client" had no idea that Juan Corrida had been murdered!

Japanese: ど‥‥どうして そんなコトがわかるのですか！
English: But how... How do you know that!?

Japanese: このグラスです。
English: From this wine glass, Your Honor.

Japanese: グラス‥‥
English: The glass...

Japanese: 霧緒さんは、イサオさんが キゼツしていると思った。
English: Mr. de Killer's supposed client thought Mr. Corrida had only fainted.

Japanese: だからこそ、このグラスに トマトジュースをそそいだ！
English: Which is why this glass of tomato juice was poured for the victim!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥しかし、それも 華宮　霧緒の計算だったのでは？
English: Hmm... But isn't that just a part of Adrian Andrews' calculated plan?

Japanese: それはあり得ません。
English: That is not possible, Your Honor.

Japanese: このグラスには、霧緒さんの 指紋が残っています。
English: This glass bears the fingerprints of that person.

Japanese: 計算された行動ならば、 自分の指紋を残すはずがない！
English: Had this been planned, they would never have left their fingerprints behind!

Japanese: ‥‥た、たしかに‥‥。 御剣検事！　いかがですか？
English: I see your point... Mr. Edgeworth!? What is your opinion?

Japanese: フシギなことに‥‥
English: Strangely enough...

Japanese: 今、私もマッタク同じことを 考えていた。
English: I had the same exact thought just now.

Japanese: ‥‥証人！　いかがだろうか、 この点について！
English: Witness! How do you explain this strange phenomenon!?

Japanese: そ、そんなことを聞かれても コマりますな。
English: I-Isn't it a waste of time to ask about such a minor detail?

Japanese: そんな細かいコトなど、 どうでもよいのでは‥‥？
English: It's not a very important point anyway, correct...?

Japanese: そうはいかない。
English: I'm afraid you are mistaken.

Japanese: ‥‥もし、華宮　霧緒が 依頼人だったのならば‥‥
English: If Adrian Andrews really is your client, as you claim...

Japanese: 当然、藤見野氏が殺害されて いるのを知っていたはずだ。
English: Then your client should have had knowledge of Mr. Corrida's death.

Japanese: 知らなかったとすれば‥‥
English: If not...

Japanese: 華宮　霧緒は依頼人ではなかった、 ということになるではないか！
English: Then that can only mean that Adrian Andrews was never your client at all!

Japanese: フシギですな‥‥。
English: How strange...

Japanese: 何が、ですかな？
English: Yes?

Japanese: なぜこの状況で、弁護士が 異議を申し立てないのか‥‥
English: Why is it that the attorney has yet to raise an objection at this absurd situation...?

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix.

Japanese: 虎狼死家に、こっちの狙いが バレたらマズいわ！
English: If De Killer figures out what we're up to, we're in real trouble!

Japanese: そ、そうですね‥‥。
English: Yeah, I know...

Japanese: 証拠もなく そんなコトを言っちゃあ‥‥
English: Mr. Edgeworth! I'm surprised!

Japanese: ダメだぞ、御剣検事。
English: You know you can't say things like that without any evidence.

Japanese: うム‥‥すまない。
English: Ah... Sorry.

Japanese: な、なんかウソっぽい “異議あり”でしたね‥‥
English: Th-That sounded like an awfully weak "Objection!" to me...

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく！　証言にムジュンが あったのは、たしかだ。
English: Anyway! I am positive there was a contradiction in that testimony.

Japanese: さらなる証言をおねがいしたい。 ‥‥依頼を受けたときのことを！
English: The prosecution requests further testimony concerning when the request was taken!

Japanese: 了解しました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: （虎狼死家は、自分のアジトに 遺留品を残して逃走した‥‥）
English: (Right now, I have to buy us more time...)

Japanese: （ぼくが今、その遺留品を待って 時間かせぎをしていること‥‥）
English: (While we wait for the items De Killer left behind to get here.)

Japanese: （それをかくし通せるかどうかで ‥‥勝負は決まる！）
English: (I just know that the very outcome of this trial lies with those items!)

Japanese: ～依頼を受けたこと～
English: -- Request Taking --

Japanese: 依頼をお受けしたのは‥‥そう、 １週間ほど前でしたか。
English: This request came to me... oh, about a week ago.

Japanese: どうしても、授賞式の夜に シゴトをしてほしいということで。
English: It was a request for my services on the night of the awards ceremony.

Japanese: そこで、とあるバーでお会いして、 ご相談をしたワケでございます。
English: We met at a certain bar to discuss and finalize a few matters.

Japanese: ええもう、キオクちがいと いうことは、あり得ません。
English: That is what occurred. I trust my memory, and I believe I have made no mistakes.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥直接、依頼人と 会っているワケですか‥‥
English: Hmm... So you physically met your client, huh?

Japanese: さよう。それが、信頼関係への 第１歩と考えますので‥‥
English: That is correct. Meeting one's client is the first step to building trust, in my opinion.

Japanese: ‥‥やれやれ。 では、弁護人。尋問を。
English: I see... Well, Mr. Wright, your cross- examination, please.

Japanese: ～依頼を受けたこと～
English: -- Request Taking --

Japanese: 依頼をお受けしたのは‥‥そう、 １週間ほど前でしたか。
English: This request came to me... oh, about a week ago.

Japanese: どうしても、授賞式の夜に シゴトをしてほしいということで。
English: It was a request for my services on the night of the awards ceremony.

Japanese: そこで、とあるバーでお会いして、 ご相談をしたワケでございます。
English: We met at a certain bar to discuss and finalize a few matters.

Japanese: ええもう、キオクちがいと いうことは、あり得ません。
English: That is what occurred. I trust my memory, and I believe I have made no mistakes.

Japanese: （とりあえず今回は、 オカシイ部分はなさそうだ‥‥）
English: (Well, there doesn't seem to be anything strange this time around...)

Japanese: なるべくシゲキしないように 情報を引き出しなさい。
English: You have to draw more information from him, but you can't draw his suspicion.

Japanese: そして、なるべくヤンワリと ムジュンをたたきつけるの。
English: If you can do that, you should be able to find a flaw in his testimony somewhere.

Japanese: （‥‥むずかしい注文だなあ‥‥）
English: (Talk about a delicate balance...)

Japanese: “この依頼人は信頼できる” ‥‥彼を見て、そう思いました。
English: From the moment I saw him, I thought, "I can trust this person as a client."

Japanese: １週間‥‥まちがい ありませんか？
English: One week ago? Are you sure?

Japanese: ええ‥‥ございません。
English: Yes, I am quite sure.

Japanese: こちらも、いろいろ 準備がありますので‥‥
English: I, of course, had my own preparations...

Japanese: ギリギリでございましたな。
English: And I was barely able to finish.

Japanese: 弁護士さんも、ご用命の際は 気をつけてくださいますよう。
English: When you request my services, Mr. Attorney, I hope you will keep that in mind.

Japanese: もう、いいですよ。 そのハナシは‥‥
English: Please... stop...

Japanese: まあ、特に今回の場合は、 日時の指定もございましたし。
English: In any case, my client this time had a very specific date and time in mind.
(A specific date and time...?)

Japanese: ‥‥その理由を 聞きましたか？
English: Did you ask why on that specific night?

Japanese: いいえ。
English: No.

Japanese: 依頼人の要求は、なるべく 聞き入れることにしております。
English: I try to fulfill all the conditions of my clients' request.

Japanese: ただ‥‥その理由について、 スイソクはしましたけれど。
English: But as for why, I only had my suspicions.

Japanese: （“スイソク”ねえ‥‥）
English: (Your suspicions, huh?)

Japanese: その“スイソク”というのは？ なぜ、授賞式の夜にシゴトを‥‥？
English: So what are these "suspicions" you had? Why did your client request that night...?

Japanese: おそらく‥‥クマ、の ためではないかと。
English: I'm sure it was all for the bear.
The bear...?

Japanese: 依頼人‥‥カミヤさまは、 こうおっしゃいました。
English: My client spoke of it.

Japanese: “現場には、かならず クマのアクセサリーがある”
English: "I'm sure there will be a bear-shaped figurine in Juan Corrida's room."

Japanese: “それを取りもどしてほしい” ‥‥と。
English: "I would like you to retrieve that item for me."

Japanese: （この、パズルの クマのことだな‥‥）
English: (He must be talking about this bear puzzle...)

Japanese: そのアクセサリーの中には、 遺書が入っていた。
English: Inside that figurine was a suicide note.

Japanese: ‥‥当然、被害者はそれを 控え室に持ってきていただろう。
English: Naturally, the victim brought it with him to his hotel room.

Japanese: ‥‥記者会見で 公表するつもりだったのだから。
English: He was planning to publicly disclose its contents at the press conference, after all.

Japanese: ‥‥さよう。
English: That is correct.

Japanese: あの夜でなければ、私には わかりませんでしたからね。
English: And if I had not done the job that night,

Japanese: クマのアクセサリーが どこにあるか‥‥
English: I would not have known where that bear figurine was...

Japanese: （なるほど‥‥）
English: (I see...)

Japanese: どうですか、弁護人。 今の証言は、重要ですか？
English: Well, Mr. Wright? Was the testimony just now of any importance?

Japanese: 今の証言で１つ、 わかったことがあります。
English: The testimony just now has made one thing clear.

Japanese: つまり‥‥
English: And that is...

Japanese: “依頼人”は、アクセサリーの ヒミツを知っていたのです！
English: The "client" knew the secret of the bear figurine!
Huh...? Why is everyone so quiet...?

Japanese: 弁護人。そんなコトは、もう とっくにわかっています。
English: Mr. Wright. I think all of us already knew that.

Japanese: そ、そうでしたっけ。
English: O-Oh, really?

Japanese: ‥‥では、証人。 つづきをおねがいしよう。
English: Witness, please continue with your testimony.

Japanese: （言ってることは もっともらしいけど‥‥）
English: (What De Killer said sounds plausible, but...)

Japanese: （ただの“スイソク” だからな‥‥）
English: (In the end, it's just his conjecture...)

Japanese: ‥‥いえ。特に 重要とは考えません。
English: No, Your Honor. I don't think it's very important.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥では、証人。 証言をつづけてください。
English: Hmm... Well then, witness, please continue.

Japanese: （殺し屋のスイソクなんて、 聞いてもしかたないか‥‥）
English: (Even if I ask about them, it would only be a guess.)

Japanese: わかりました。 もう、けっこうです。
English: I see... That's fine then.

Japanese: 直接、会ったんですね？ 華宮　霧緒さんに。
English: So you physically met Adrian Andrews, right?

Japanese: モチロンでございます。
English: Of course I did.

Japanese: （なんだ？　今、ちょっと 口ごもったような‥‥）
English: (What was that?? What was with the brief pause...?)

Japanese: 証人！　‥‥もっと、 詳しくおねがいします。
English: Witness! I would like for you to give us a few more details.

Japanese: ‥‥キホン的に、私はかならず 依頼人に会うことにしています。
English: I always meet my clients as a matter of principle.

Japanese: 電話や手紙で依頼を受ける ことはありません。
English: I have never taken a request by telephone or mail.

Japanese: それは、なぜか‥‥？
English: And why is that?

Japanese: 私には、依頼人との信頼関係が イチバン重要ですから‥‥
English: That's because I value the trust between a client and myself above all else.

Japanese: やはり、お客さまの目を見て お話ししたいワケでございます。
English: And the only way to establish that is to speak to the client while looking them in the eye.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: どうですか、弁護人。 今の証言は、重要ですか？
English: Well, Mr. Wright? Was the testimony just now of any importance?

Japanese: それはモチロン、重要です。
English: Of course, it was very important, Your Honor.

Japanese: 事前に依頼人と会っていれば、 カンちがいなどあり得ません！
English: If Mr. de Killer had met his client before the murder, then it's unlikely he is mistaken!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: ではやはり、依頼人は 華宮　霧緒さんだったワケですか。
English: So you're saying that his client really was Adrian Andrews?

Japanese: あ‥‥え、ええ。 そうなりますね‥‥。
English: Ah... um... I guess so...

Japanese: ‥‥ホラ。申し上げた とおりでしょう？
English: You see. It is just as I said.
Gnngh!

Japanese: （ぼくはいったい、どっちの ミカタなんだっけ‥‥？）
English: (I'm so lost... Who the heck am I supposed to be helping here...?)

Japanese: 落ちついて、なるほどくん。 ‥‥じっくり考えてみるの。
English: Calm down, Phoenix. Think carefully and relax.

Japanese: では、証人。 つづきをおねがいします。
English: Now then, would the witness please continue?

Japanese: “依頼人と会う理由”‥‥ そんなことは重要じゃない。
English: Why he meets his clients is not important. And that wasn't the point.

Japanese: ‥‥証人。ぼくの質問を はぐらかさないでください。
English: Witness, please stop side- stepping my questions!

Japanese: ど、どういうイミですか、 それは‥‥
English: Wh-What do you mean by that?

Japanese: “本当に霧緒さんと会ったのか？” ‥‥聞きたいのは、この一点です。
English: My question was "Did you really meet Adrian Andrews in person?"

Japanese: だから‥‥申しあげたでしょう。 お会いした、と。
English: I have already told you, Mr. Wright. I did.

Japanese: 目を見てハナシを聞いて、 私は彼を信用したワケです。
English: It was only through talking with him face to face that I began to trust him.

Japanese: “この依頼人は、信頼できる” ‥‥と感じたワケでございますな。
English: That's when I thought, "I can trust this person as a client."

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ そういうものですかね。
English: Hmm... It's true what they say about talking face to face.

Japanese: ‥‥いかがですか、弁護人。 この証言は、重要ですか？
English: Well, Mr. Wright? Was the testimony just now of any importance?

Japanese: （‥‥ぼくの耳に まちがいがなければ‥‥）
English: (If I heard what I think I heard just now...)

Japanese: （ついに、つかまえたな）
English: (Then I think I've got him.)

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。今の発言は ものすごく重要です。
English: Your Honor. I believe the testimony just now was of the utmost importance.

Japanese: え。そうでしたか？
English: Huh? Really?

Japanese: それならば‥‥証人。
English: If that's the case...

Japanese: 今の発言を証言に くわえていただきたい。
English: Witness, please include the statement just now in your testimony.

Japanese: はあ‥‥わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: （きっと、何かあると 思ったんだけどな‥‥）
English: (I was so sure there was something, but I can't get a good grip on what it is...)

Japanese: そうですね。特に重要では ないと思います。
English: Hmm, I guess it wasn't all that important.

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix.

Japanese: ‥‥は、はい。
English: Y-Yes?

Japanese: あなた、何を聞いていたの？
English: What did you hear just now?
Um...

Japanese: ‥‥まだまだね。 これを聞き逃すなんて‥‥
English: Clean out your earwax and pay attention, Phoenix. You're letting his words slip by!

Japanese: （な、なんかシッパイ したのか‥‥？　ぼく）
English: (Um... I guess I missed something back there...?)

Japanese: ‥‥では、証人。 証言をつづけていただこう。
English: Witness, please continue with your testimony.

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥ 気のせいだろう）
English: (It's probably just my imagination...)

Japanese: （もっと、決定的にアヤシイ ところを探すんだ！）
English: (I need to find something more definitive to catch this guy on!)

Japanese: ‥‥今までの証言、 たしかなんですね？
English: Can we believe that your testimony up to this point has been reliable?

Japanese: 依頼人は、華宮　霧緒さん‥‥
English: So your client was Adrian Andrews...?

Japanese: さようでございます。
English: That is correct.

Japanese: （‥‥依頼するとき、 ２人は会ってるらしい‥‥）
English: (Well, he says the two of them met...)

Japanese: （それなら、“キオクちがい”は ないかもしれないな‥‥）
English: (But if they did, then there shouldn't be anything wrong with De Killer's testimony...)

Japanese: でも、結果的には そうではなかったわけですね？
English: But as we now know, that was not how it turned out, correct?

Japanese: どういうことですか？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: だって、霧緒さんは“仁義”に はずれた依頼人だったんでしょう？
English: Adrian Andrews turned out to be a client who couldn't stick to the rules, right?

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、たしかに‥‥。
English: ...Well, yes.

Japanese: たまには、 そういうこともあります。
English: I suppose you are correct.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 最後にカクニンする。 証言にまちがいはあるまいな。
English: So I would like to check one last time. Are you sure your testimony is accurate?

Japanese: ‥‥もう一度、 カクニンさせてください。
English: I would like to go over this one more time.

Japanese: あなたは、バーでカミヤ　キリオに 会って、依頼を受けたのですね？
English: You met Adrian Andrews at a bar and took the request at that time?

Japanese: そうでございますよ、ええ。
English: Yes, that is correct.

Japanese: そして‥‥“彼”のことを 信頼できると考えた‥‥。
English: And that's when you thought "he" was trustworthy...

Japanese: さよう。何度も そう申し上げております。
English: How many times must I repeat myself? Yes, that is correct.

Japanese: ‥‥ザンネンながら‥‥ そんなことはあり得ないんですよ。
English: I'm sorry, but that is an impossible tale.

Japanese: な‥‥なんでございますか？
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: 虎狼死家　左々右エ門。
English: Shelly de Killer.

Japanese: あなたは、カミヤ　キリオ本人に 会ったことはない！
English: You have never met the real Adrian Andrews!

Japanese: ど‥‥どうして、 そんなことが‥‥？
English: Wh-Why would you say that...?

Japanese: あなたは‥‥決定的な カンちがいをしているんですよ。
English: Because you made one very big slip-up...

Japanese: ‥‥彼女について。
English: ...about her.

Japanese: だ、だから それはいったい、なんだと‥‥
English: So what is the issue...

Japanese: 今‥‥なんと おっしゃいましたか？
English: Wh-What did you say just now...?

Japanese: ‥‥“彼女について”‥‥？
English: About "her"...?

Japanese: もし、あなたが霧緒さんに 会ったのなら‥‥
English: If you had ever met Adrian Andrews in person...

Japanese: 一目でわかったはずです。 ‥‥彼女が、女性であると！
English: One look would have told you that she is a woman!

Japanese: おほオォォォォッ！
English: Ohoooooo!

Japanese: せ、静粛に！　静粛に！ これは‥‥どういうことですかッ！
English: O-Order! Order in the court! Mr. Wright! What is the meaning of this!?

Japanese: この証人は、ハッキリと こう証言しています。
English: This witness testified to the following:

Japanese: “依頼を受けるときは、 本人と会って話を聞く”と！
English: That he always meets face to face with his clients when taking their request.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし、彼は華宮　霧緒と 会っていなかった‥‥
English: But he has never met Adrian Andrews in person...

Japanese: それならば！
English: Yes, Your Honor! That is exactly the point!

Japanese: 依頼人は、華宮　霧緒では なかったということになる！
English: That means Mr. de Killer's client could not have been Ms. Adrian Andrews!

Japanese: ‥‥んむうぅゥゥゥゥ‥‥
English: Ugnnn...

Japanese: 御剣検事‥‥ あなたの主張はリカイできます。
English: Mr. Edgeworth... I understand your logic on this one...

Japanese: しかし‥‥
English: However...

Japanese: どうして殺し屋は、 そんなカンちがいを‥‥？
English: Why would the assassin make such a basic mistake?

Japanese: おそらく‥‥ 名前のせいではないだろうか。
English: I believe it has to do with her name, Your Honor.

Japanese: なまえ‥‥？
English: Her name?

Japanese: カミヤ　キリオという名前からは ‥‥“男性”がイメージされる。
English: Yes. Adrian Andrews is, without a doubt, a very androgynous name.

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥まあ、たしかに。
English: Hmm... Yes, I see...

Japanese: そして、幸か不幸か‥‥
English: Unluckily for Mr. de Killer,

Japanese: 交信が始まってから、華宮　霧緒の 性別に関する発言は‥‥
English: the entire time he was on the stand, no one had stated Adrian Andrews' gender.

Japanese: ただの１つも、なかったのだ。
English: And so, he simply picked the wrong gender to go with.
Wh-What...

Japanese: なんということでしょう‥‥
English: What is going on...?

Japanese: ‥‥虎狼死家　左々右エ門！
English: Shelly de Killer!

Japanese: ナットクのいく 説明をおねがいしましょうか。
English: This court demands an explanation!

Japanese: ‥‥ううむ‥‥
English: Umm...

Japanese: ど、どうやら‥‥キオクが コンランしていたようです。
English: I-I think somehow... I must have mixed up this client with another.

Japanese: というと‥‥今は 思い出したということか？
English: So does that mean you remember something different now?

Japanese: もちろんでございます。
English: Yes, of course.

Japanese: もう一度、 証言をさせていただければ‥‥
English: Please, if you would allow me to testify once more...

Japanese: （くそ‥‥また、 ウソを重ねるつもりだな‥‥）
English: (Argh! I know he's just going to spit out more lies.)

Japanese: わかりました。‥‥では、 もう一度、証言をおねがいします！
English: Very well. But this time, please give us the truth, and nothing but the truth!

Japanese: ～依頼を受けたこと・２～
English: -- Request Taking, Pt. 2 --

Japanese: ‥‥さよう。依頼は 手紙によって受けました。
English: Yes, now I remember. I took that request by mail.

Japanese: 依頼人と会わないで契約することも ございますもので。
English: There have been times when I took a job without having met my client.

Japanese: 藤見野　イサオの殺害と、２、３の 細かいシゴトを依頼されました。
English: The request was for the murder of Juan Corrida and 2 or 3 other small things.

Japanese: お名前を見て“男性なのだ”と 思いこんでしまったのでしょうな。
English: When I saw the name at the end of the letter, I thought my client to be a man.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥手紙で依頼を受けた‥‥
English: Hmm... So you took this job through a letter...

Japanese: （さっきは、手紙のことなんて ヒトコトも言ってなかった‥‥）
English: (He didn't mention anything about a letter in his earlier testimony...)

Japanese: （‥‥ウソに決まってる！）
English: (Which means he is definitely lying!)

Japanese: ‥‥気をつけて。なるほどくん。
English: Be careful, Phoenix.

Japanese: 殺し屋を、カンペキに 追いつめてしまったらオシマイよ。
English: If you break the assassin's testimony completely, it's over for us.

Japanese: わかっています。 ‥‥でも‥‥
English: I know... I can't make him suspicious. But...

Japanese: なんとなく‥‥“だいじょうぶだ” って、感じるんです。
English: I think we're OK; like we can do this.

Japanese: （あの男が あそこにいるかぎり）
English: (As long as he's standing there across from me...)

Japanese: （どんなキワドイ攻撃でも、 きっと反撃してくる‥‥）
English: (No matter how strong of a punch I throw, he'll counter it...)

Japanese: では、尋問をおねがいします。
English: Now then, let's begin the cross-examination.

Japanese: ～依頼を受けたこと・２～
English: -- Request Taking, Pt. 2 --

Japanese: ‥‥さよう。依頼は 手紙によって受けました。
English: Yes, now I remember. I took that request by mail.

Japanese: 依頼人と会わないで契約することも ございますもので。
English: There have been times when I took a job without having met my client.

Japanese: 藤見野　イサオの殺害と、２、３の 細かいシゴトを依頼されました。
English: The request was for the murder of Juan Corrida and 2 or 3 other small things.

Japanese: お名前を見て“男性なのだ”と 思いこんでしまったのでしょうな。
English: When I saw the name at the end of the letter, I thought my client to be a man.

Japanese: そろそろ‥‥限界ね。
English: We've pretty much reached the end of our rope here...

Japanese: え？　でも。 ケッコウ調子いいですよ。
English: Huh? Seems like we're still OK to me.

Japanese: だから、マズいのよ。
English: And that's exactly what is so bad.

Japanese: このままだと、あの殺し屋さんの ウソをカンゼンに暴いてしまう。
English: At the rate we're going, we will end up completely destroying De Killer's lie.

Japanese: そうなると‥‥サイアクの 事態をまねくわ。
English: If we do that... You already know how serious of a situation that will put us in.

Japanese: あ‥‥そうか。
English: O-Oh yeah...

Japanese: （‥‥ヤツの遺留品がとどくのを 祈るしかないのか‥‥）
English: (All I can do now is pray that those items reach us in time...)

Japanese: クマのアクセサリーを探して、 カミヤさまにわたしました。
English: One of these was to find the bear figurine and to give it to Adrian Andrews.

Japanese: しかし‥‥さっきは、 かならず依頼人に会う、と‥‥
English: But didn't you just say that you always meet your clients?

Japanese: 少し前までは そうだったのですが‥‥
English: Yes, I suppose I did say that.

Japanese: お客さまの中には、どうしても 会えないという方もおりまして。
English: However, there are some clients for whom a meeting is simply not possible.

Japanese: でも‥‥今回の依頼人とは、 会ったのではないですか？
English: But didn't you meet your client this time?

Japanese: 会っておりません。
English: No, I did not.

Japanese: またまた、そんな‥‥ イジを張るの、やめましょうよ。
English: Oh, come now. Let's stop with this game of cat and mouse.

Japanese: ネコなで声を出してもムダです。
English: Using your silkiest voice is not going to work on me.

Japanese: そこを、なんとか！
English: Alright then, just cough it up and confess!

Japanese: “そこを、なんとか”は ダメでしょう、成歩堂くん！
English: Mr. Wright! You can't badger a witness with such harsh words!

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: Um...

Japanese: いちおう弁護士なのですから、 証拠でカタをつけるように。
English: You're a lawyer, so behave like one and present evidence instead of mindlessly yelling.

Japanese: 依頼人と虎狼死家が、会った という証拠は、ありますか？
English: Now then, do you have any proof that Mr. de Killer met with his client?

Japanese: （ここまで来たら、 なんとかして立証してやる！）
English: (We've come this far! I have to try to prove something here!)

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 では、お見せしましょう。
English: Very well, I will show you proof.

Japanese: だいじょうぶなの？ なるほどくん‥‥
English: Are you sure about this, Phoenix?

Japanese: イサオさん殺害の依頼人と 証人が会ったという証拠は‥‥！
English: Here is the proof that the witness met with his client, who wished Mr. Corrida dead!

Japanese: さあ！　いかがですか！
English: So! What do you think!?

Japanese: “いかがですか”と 言われましても‥‥
English: You may say, "What do you think?"...

Japanese: なにぶん、トランシーバー なものでして、私。
English: However, I am for all intents and purposes, a transceiver radio.

Japanese: あ。見えませんね‥‥証拠品。
English: Oh... That's right, you can't actually see the evidence...

Japanese: まあ‥‥見なくとも わかりますが。
English: Well, I don't think this was one I needed to see.

Japanese: そちらの、 シラけきった空気は。
English: I can hear the pure silence in the air there.

Japanese: （くそッ！ ちがったらしいな‥‥）
English: (Argh! How could I have picked the wrong piece at a time like this!?)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥では、 証言をつづけてもらいましょう。
English: Hmm... In any case, let's continue with the testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥ザンネンですが‥‥ 証拠はありません。
English: I'm sorry, Your Honor. Unfortunately, I don't have any proof.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥そうですか‥‥
English: Hmm, I see.

Japanese: それならば、これ以上の追求は イミがありませんな。
English: Then your line of questioning was just another waste of time.

Japanese: ‥‥しかたがない。 証言をつづけていただこう。
English: Sadly for us, Your Honor, that is the nature of Wright and wrong.

Japanese: ‥‥どうしてですか？
English: And why could you not meet certain clients?

Japanese: 最近、依頼のほうが 増えてまいりまして。
English: Recently, I have been receiving more requests.

Japanese: いちいち会っていては、 ビジネスチャンスを逃します。
English: If I met each and every client, I would lose some nice business opportunities.

Japanese: ‥‥びじねすちゃんす‥‥
English: Nice business opportunities?

Japanese: それにまあ、こういう ご時世ですから。
English: On top of which, the times have changed.

Japanese: パーソナル・コンピュータ というのでございますか？
English: It is now the age of information and computers, correct?

Japanese: 私も、エレクトロニック・メールに よる依頼の受付を始めたワケです。
English: Well I have joined the times, and now take requests via electronic mail.

Japanese: ‥‥えれくとろにっくめーる‥‥
English: Electronic mail? Do you have to mail that in a special insulated envelope?

Japanese: あ、申しわけございません。
English: Ah, I'm very sorry.

Japanese: 私、コトバをみじかく ちぢめるのがキライでして‥‥
English: I despise the shortening of words.

Japanese: モチロン、電子メールのコトで ございます。
English: What I meant by electronic mail is what is commonly referred to as "e-mail".

Japanese: ‥‥でんしめーる‥‥
English: E-mail...?

Japanese: （裁判長‥‥さっきから オウムになり下がってるな‥‥）
English: (In a contest of mimicry, the judge would beat a parrot, hands down...)

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく。
English: *ahem*

Japanese: 証人は、依頼人と会わずに シゴトを受けたわけだな‥‥？
English: Anyway, so you took this job without having met your client, and...?

Japanese: ２、３の細かいシゴト‥‥。
English: Two or three other things...?

Japanese: さよう。
English: Yes.

Japanese: ‥‥それは、 なんのことですか？
English: And what were these "other things"?

Japanese: 事件にはカンケイない、 ささいなコトでございます。
English: A few other things that have nothing to do with this case.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: （どうする‥‥。 聞き出すべきだろうか？）
English: (What should I do...? Should I let him slide with that...?)

Japanese: （ヘタにシゲキして、 怒らせてもマズいけど‥‥）
English: (It'd be really bad if I push his buttons the wrong way and he gets mad...)

Japanese: 事件に関係あるかどうかは、 こちらで判断します。
English: Whether or not they're related to this case is for the court to decide.

Japanese: ‥‥弁護士さん‥‥
English: ...Mr. Attorney.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですか？
English: Y-Yes?

Japanese: 私はさっきから、あなたの依頼人に 有利なコトを証言しています。
English: Everything I have said from the beginning has been nothing but beneficial to your client.

Japanese: それなのになぜ‥‥こんな尋問を つづけるのですか？
English: Which is why I wonder what is pushing you to continue with this cross-examination.

Japanese: もしかしてあなたは‥‥
English: Could it be...

Japanese: ご自分の依頼人を、 ウラ切ろうとしているのでは‥‥？
English: That you are planning to betray your own client...?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥
English: Th-That's...

Japanese: 私は、ウラ切りというものを 最もニクんでいます。
English: I smell the stench of a back- stabber.

Japanese: そちらが その気なのであれば‥‥
English: And should you turn out to be one...

Japanese: ままま、待ってくださいッ！
English: W-W-Wait!

Japanese: （マズいな‥‥コーフン状態だ！）
English: (Uh oh... This is looking really bad!)

Japanese: （本当に重要なコトでないかぎり、 深追いはキケンだぞ‥‥）
English: (I shouldn't press my luck. Alright, I have to think. Is this worth pursuing...?)

Japanese: ‥‥証人。これは 重要なことなのです。
English: Witness, this is a very important matter.

Japanese: 証言をおねがいします。依頼人に たのまれた“シゴト”とは‥‥？
English: Please cooperate and tell us what these other "jobs" your client requested were...

Japanese: そうなのですか。 ‥‥それなら、やむを得ませんね。
English: If it's truly that important, I suppose I don't have much of a choice.

Japanese: クマのアクセサリーです。
English: The bear figurine.

Japanese: クマの‥‥？
English: The bear figurine...?

Japanese: ターゲットを殺害後、私は アクセサリーを探しました。
English: After the assassination of the target, I was to find that figurine.

Japanese: 殺害と同じぐらい重要なコトだ ‥‥そう言われていましたので。
English: I was told that this job was just as important as the actual killing.

Japanese: それで‥‥ そのアクセサリーは‥‥？
English: And... Where was that figurine...?

Japanese: 彼のトランクの中に 入っておりました。
English: It was inside Mr. Corrida's suitcase.

Japanese: それで‥‥それを どうしましたか？
English: And then... what did you do next?

Japanese: まちがいなく、わたしましたよ。 私の依頼人に‥‥。
English: I handed it over to my client right away.

Japanese: “依頼人”に‥‥わたした‥‥
English: You gave it to your "client"... Interesting.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥たしかに、 それは重要なコトですな。
English: Hmm... This information certainly sounds important to me.

Japanese: ‥‥証人。今の発言を、 証言につけくわえてください。
English: Witness, please include what you just stated in your testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥かしこまりました。
English: As you wish.

Japanese: （虎狼死家のヤツ‥‥ なかなかスルドイからな）
English: (De Killer sounds like one sharp man...)

Japanese: （‥‥とりあえず、 他の手がかりを探してみよう）
English: (I should try to find a better way to do this without making him suspicious.)

Japanese: では、証人。 ‥‥証言をつづけてください。
English: Let's continue with the testimony. Witness, if you please.

Japanese: （虎狼死家のヤツ‥‥ なかなかスルドイからな）
English: (De Killer sounds like one sharp man...)

Japanese: （‥‥とりあえず、 他の手がかりを探してみよう）
English: (I should try to find a better way to do this without making him suspicious.)

Japanese: では、証人。 ‥‥証言をつづけてください。
English: Let's continue with the testimony. Witness, if you please.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、依頼人のカオは、 一度も見なかったワケですか？
English: So you're saying that you never saw your client's face. Not even once?

Japanese: 一度だけ‥‥
English: I did... Once.

Japanese: お部屋にうかがって、 アクセサリーをわたしました。
English: It was when I went to give my client the figurine.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥そうでしたね。
English: Hmm. Yes, I see.

Japanese: しかし、そのときカミヤさまは ‥‥お面をかぶっておりました。
English: But Ms. Andrews was wearing a mask at the time.

Japanese: おめん‥‥？
English: A mask?

Japanese: （トノサマンのマスク、か）
English: (The Nickel Samurai mask, I'm guessing...?)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥いかがですか？ 弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright... What do you have to say about this?

Japanese: 今の証言に、おかしなところが ありますかな？
English: Do you have any problems with this piece of testimony?

Japanese: ‥‥１つだけ、 気になることがあります。
English: One thing does sort of stick out at me, Your Honor.

Japanese: 証人。あなたは、依頼人の スガオを見ているハズなんです。
English: Witness. I think you most definitely saw your client's face.

Japanese: 荷星さんの証言を 思い出してください。
English: Let's recall Mr. Powers' testimony.

Japanese: 授賞式が終わってから、１人で 控え室へ行ったんです。
English: After the award ceremony, I went by myself to Matt's room.

Japanese: 控え室の前に、オートロくんが。 トノサマンの衣装を着てましたね。
English: Matt was standing there in front of his room, still in his Nickel Samurai costume.

Japanese: オートロくん‥‥ボーイさんに チップをあげたんですよ。
English: Matt gave the bellboy a tip.

Japanese: ‥‥あなたは、王都楼さんから 札束を受けとっている！
English: You received quite a large roll of cash from Mr. Engarde!

Japanese: そのとき、彼はトノサマンの マスクを脱いでいたハズです！
English: And at the time, he was not wearing his Nickel Samurai mask!

Japanese: おほオォォォォッ！
English: Ohoooooo!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！ そういえば、そうでしたな‥‥
English: Order! Order! Yes, now that you mention it, I do remember that...

Japanese: いかがですか！　証人。
English: Witness!

Japanese: 私はたしかに、ボーイとして あの会場におりました。
English: Yes, that night I did wander the floor as a bellboy.

Japanese: ジュースを運んで、チップを たくさん、もらいました。
English: I received plenty of tips that night for carrying juice to the various rooms.

Japanese: それの、どこが悪いのですか？
English: Is that so wrong?
Huh?

Japanese: 私にチップをくれた方は、 依頼人ではありません。
English: The man who gave me that tip was not my client.

Japanese: ただ‥‥ちょっと気前の いいヒトだったのでしょう。
English: He was probably just a very generous person.

Japanese: ‥‥ザンネンながら、われわれは そこまで気前はよくない‥‥。
English: I'm sorry, but sadly, we are not nearly so generous here.

Japanese: お盆を運んでサツタバが もらえるのならば‥‥！
English: If I could receive large rolls of cash by simply bringing people things on trays...

Japanese: ダレが検事などやるものかッ！
English: Then why on Earth would I stand around here prosecuting!?

Japanese: （あいつ‥‥検事の給料に マンゾクしてないのか‥‥？）
English: (Oh, the déjà vu... And isn't his salary more than enough for one man...?)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: しかし、その札束が チップでなかったという証拠は‥‥
English: And where is your evidence that the large roll of cash was not in fact, a tip...?

Japanese: 今のところ、 ありませんからねえ。
English: Come, Mr. Edgeworth. Show me the money!

Japanese: ナニィィィィィッ！
English: Whaaaat!?

Japanese: ‥‥弁護士さん。
English: Mr. Attorney.

Japanese: な、なんでしょう？
English: Y-Yes?

Japanese: やはり、あなたの質問は、 さきほどからオカシイ‥‥。
English: You know, I think your line of questioning has been a little strange...

Japanese: まるで、カミヤさまが依頼人と 認めるつもりがないように見える。
English: In fact, I would say you don't seem to believe Ms. Andrews is my client.

Japanese: そ、そんなコトありませんよ。 ただ、ウソはよくないなあ、と‥‥
English: Oh, no, it's not like that at all. I just think lies aren't a good thing, you know...?

Japanese: そのとおり。 ‥‥ウソはよろしくない‥‥
English: Oh, I know and agree. Lies are not a good thing at all...

Japanese: う。
English: Urk.

Japanese: よろしいですね‥‥弁護士さん。
English: I think we are on the same page now, aren't we Mr. Attorney?

Japanese: ‥‥もし私をダマそうと しておられるなら‥‥
English: Remember. If I feel threatened in any way...

Japanese: こんな交信‥‥いつ中断しても よろしいのですよ？
English: I am free to cut contact at any time...

Japanese: す、スミマセン！ ‥‥ぼくがバカでした！
English: I'm sorry! Please forgive my foolishness!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥いつも こうスナオならいいのですが。
English: Hmm... If only you were this apologetic all the time...

Japanese: とにかく、これは決定的な ムジュンとは認められません。
English: Anyway, I do not see a huge contradiction here.

Japanese: ‥‥証人。証言に もどってください。
English: Therefore, you may continue witness.

Japanese: （‥‥なんとなく 引っかかるけど‥‥）
English: (I think I can pull something out of what he said...)

Japanese: （ささいな点に こだわるのはキケンだ！）
English: (But it would be real bad if I did something and made him mad over something trivial!)

Japanese: ‥‥証言には 問題ないと思います。
English: There are no problems with the testimony, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: ぼくは、そのアクセサリーを 王都楼さんの屋敷で見つけました。
English: I found this figurine at Mr. Engarde's mansion.

Japanese: 霧緒さんにわたしたのなら、 なぜ、そんなところに‥‥？
English: If you gave it to Ms. Andrews, then what was it doing there?

Japanese: おそらく、彼女が 持ちこんだのでしょうな。
English: I was waiting for her there.

Japanese: モチロン、王都楼さまに 罪を着せるために‥‥。
English: That was also part of the plan to frame Mr. Engarde, I'm sure...

Japanese: ふむう‥‥スジは とおっているようですね。
English: Hmm... That makes a lot of sense.

Japanese: いかがですか？　弁護人。 今の証言は‥‥
English: Well, Mr. Wright? Do you have any problems with this piece of testimony?

Japanese: （“虎狼死家は、霧緒さんに アクセサリーをわたした”）
English: (So De Killer says he gave the figurine to Ms. Andrews.)

Japanese: （この証言‥‥どこか ムジュンしている気がする‥‥）
English: (But I know somewhere in that statement there is a contradiction...)

Japanese: （しかし‥‥）
English: (And yet...)

Japanese: （決定的なムジュンを 示せなかった場合は‥‥）
English: (I know that if I present something trivial here...)

Japanese: ‥‥交信を中断される おそれがあるわ。
English: ...He will cut the connection on his end.

Japanese: ハッキリしたムジュンを 示すつもりならば‥‥
English: If you want to make a strong point, Phoenix...

Japanese: 証拠品をつきつけるしかない。
English: You have to present strong evidence.

Japanese: （そのとおりだ。 さあどうする、成歩堂　龍一！）
English: (She's right. So now what, Dr. Wright!?)

Japanese: ‥‥証人。 もう一度、カクニンします。
English: Witness, let's go over this one more time.

Japanese: “霧緒さんに、クマの アクセサリーをわたした”
English: You gave Ms. Andrews the bear figurine.

Japanese: “王都楼さんの屋敷にそれを 持ちこんだのは、霧緒さんだ”
English: And she told you to take the bear and wait for her at Engarde Mansion.

Japanese: ‥‥それが、 あなたの主張ですね？
English: Is that correct?

Japanese: そうでございますが‥‥？
English: Yes... Where are you going with this...?

Japanese: ‥‥たぶん、それは 証人のキオクちがいですね。
English: Well, I think maybe you might have remembered a few things incorrectly.

Japanese: な、なんですと‥‥！
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: （さあ‥‥ここが勝負どころ。 ハズすワケには行かない！）
English: (This is a battle of wits! I can't let up on him!)

Japanese: 霧緒さんが、クマのアクセサリーを あなたから受けとったハズはない！
English: I don't think it is possible for Ms. Andrews to have been the recipient of this bear!

Japanese: さあ！　いかがですか！
English: So! What do you think!?

Japanese: 証人ッ！
English: Witness!

Japanese: コロシヤさん！
English: Mr. de Killer!

Japanese: ‥‥あ。どうも シツレイいたしました。
English: Oh, I'm sorry.

Japanese: トイレに行っていたもので。
English: I went to visit the water closet for a second.
...Huh?

Japanese: ‥‥弁護士さん。そろそろ ハッキリさせてもらいたいですね。
English: Mr. Attorney, I think it's time I stated this in terms even you can comprehend.

Japanese: これ以上、ムダな質問が あった場合は‥‥
English: If you ask me any more of these pointless questions...

Japanese: ヨウシャしませんぞッ！
English: There will be no mercy!
Urk!

Japanese: ‥‥では、証言を つづけさせていただきます。
English: Now, I would like to move on with my testimony.
(It's no use...)

Japanese: （核心をつくムジュンに 気がつかないかぎり‥‥）
English: (As long as I can't put my finger on the central problem here,)

Japanese: （この証人をゆさぶるのは キケンすぎる‥‥！）
English: (pressing this witness any more would be extremely dangerous!)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥どうやら モンダイなし、ですかな。
English: Hmm... It appears that Mr. Wright has no problems.

Japanese: では、証人。 ‥‥証言をつづけてください。
English: Well then, witness. Please continue.

Japanese: ‥‥虎狼死家　左々右エ門。
English: Shelly de Killer.

Japanese: もしあなたが、コイツを 霧緒さんにわたしたのなら‥‥
English: If you had really given the bear to Ms. Andrews...

Japanese: その中に、こんなものが 入っているワケがないんですよ。
English: then this item should not have been inside it.

Japanese: “こんなもの”‥‥？
English: "This item"...?

Japanese: ‥‥そういうコト、か‥‥
English: I see where you're going...

Japanese: ‥‥そういうコトです。
English: Yup, that's where I'm going...

Japanese: どういうコトですかッ！
English: Where is everyone going!? Do I need to pack a suitcase?

Japanese: ‥‥霧緒さんの証言を 思い出してください。
English: Your Honor. Please think back to Ms. Andrews' testimony.
