Japanese: 彼女のためにも‥‥ 燃やしてしまいたかった。
English: And I was going to burn it... for her sake.

Japanese: もし、たとえそれが １分間だったとしても！
English: If even for a single minute,

Japanese: 霧緒さんが、このアクセサリーを 手にしたのならば‥‥
English: this bear had actually been in Ms. Andrews' hands...

Japanese: 彼女が、遺書を 取り出さなかったハズがない！
English: I'm sure she would have taken the suicide note out and burned it!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ ‥‥そういうコトですかッ！
English: Order! Order! Order! So that's where you two were going!

Japanese: それなのに‥‥こうして、 遺書が残っていたということは‥‥
English: So by the very fact that this suicide note was still inside the bear...

Japanese: “依頼人”は、このパズルの 解き方を知らなかったのだッ！
English: Tells us that your "client" didn't know how to disassemble the puzzle!

Japanese: つ、つまり‥‥？
English: Wh-Which means...?

Japanese: 依頼人は華宮　霧緒ではなかった、 という可能性が残される！
English: It means, Your Honor, that it is impossible for Adrian Andrews to be the "client"!

Japanese: おほオォォォォッ！
English: Ohoooooo!

Japanese: せ‥‥静粛に！　静粛に！ 静まりなさいッ！
English: O...Order! Order! ORDER!!

Japanese: ‥‥グッ‥‥な‥‥ 成歩堂　龍一‥‥
English: Ungh... Mr...Phoenix Wright...

Japanese: も‥‥申しあげたハズです。
English: I... I'm sure I mentioned this before.

Japanese: 私はウラ切り者を 最もニクむ、と‥‥
English: How I hate traitors above all else!

Japanese: どうやら‥‥今の尋問で ハッキリしたようです。
English: I think your cross-examination has clearly demonstrated something to me.

Japanese: あなたは‥‥私との 約束をやぶるおつもりらしい！
English: You... You must wish to break your end of our agreement!

Japanese: いや！　そ、それは‥‥
English: No! That's not...

Japanese: よろしい！　それならば、 私のするべきコトは、１つです！
English: That's enough! If that is your intention, then there is only one thing for me to do!

Japanese: ま‥‥待ってくれッ！
English: W-Wait! Please!

Japanese: ‥‥私はこれで シツレイします。
English: Gentlemen, ladies, please excuse me.

Japanese: かたづけなければならない シゴトがありますので‥‥
English: I have a matter that I must attend to.

Japanese: た‥‥たのむ。 それだけは‥‥待ってくれ‥‥
English: N...No... Please... Not that... Please wait...

Japanese: それならば！
English: Mr. Attorney!

Japanese: この審理を、さっさと 終わらせることです！
English: Bring this trial to a speedy end, and I may stay my hand! Otherwise...
Nnnngh...

Japanese: うおおおおおおおおおおおおおッ！
English: Gnwaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaahhh!!

Japanese: ‥‥いったい‥‥ どうしたのですか？　弁護人。
English: What in the...? Mr. Wright...? Are you...?

Japanese: ‥‥御剣検事？
English: Mr. Edgeworth...?

Japanese: う‥‥うム。
English: Yes, Your Honor?

Japanese: ‥‥どうも、この証人の発言には ナットクのいかない点があります。
English: I didn't understand this witness' outburst just now.

Japanese: さらに証言を聞く必要があると 思いますが‥‥いかがですか？
English: Do you think there is a need to hear more testimony, or is this enough...?

Japanese: そ、そうだな。 ‥‥モチロン‥‥
English: Well... We should...

Japanese: （ダメだ‥‥御剣！）
English: (Edgeworth! We can't do this!)

Japanese: （これ以上つづけたら ‥‥真宵ちゃんが‥‥！）
English: (If we keep this up... Maya... She'll...!)

Japanese: う‥‥ぐぐグッ‥‥！
English: U...Ungh!

Japanese: け‥‥検察側は‥‥私は‥‥ I...
English: The prosecution...

Japanese: ど、どうしたのですか？ あなたまで‥‥
English: Wh-What has come over everyone? Even you are...

Japanese: し‥‥質問はない。
English: The prosecution... rests.

Japanese: どういうことですか‥‥？
English: What is going on around here...?

Japanese: これで、検察側からの 質問を‥‥‥‥終了したい。
English: The prosecution has no further questions, Your Honor.
Wh...

Japanese: なんですってえええェェェッ！
English: Whaaaaaaat!?

Japanese: ‥‥きわめて 疑わしい状況ですが‥‥
English: Well, I never thought I'd see the day. This is a most unusual situation...

Japanese: このまま検察側の質問が 終わるのであれば、
English: If the prosecution rests with no further questions...

Japanese: 検察側の立証は‥‥失敗、 ということになります。
English: Then... the prosecution has failed to uphold its stance.

Japanese: そうなると‥‥ まことに不本意ながら、
English: If that is the case, then even though I am reluctant,

Japanese: 殺し屋の証言は正しかった、と 考えざるを得ません。
English: I must believe that Mr. de Killer's testimony is accurate.

Japanese: 虎狼死家　左々右エ門の “依頼人”は、やはり‥‥
English: That would mean that Shelly de Killer's client is...

Japanese: 華宮　霧緒だった、 ということになります！
English: Adrian Andrews!

Japanese: ‥‥うむむむ‥‥
English: Nnngh...

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: は‥‥はい。
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor?

Japanese: 今、ここで 審理を終了するならば‥‥
English: If I end the trial here, right now,

Japanese: あなたの依頼人、 王都楼　真悟は無罪になります。
English: then your client, Matt Engarde, would be declared innocent.

Japanese: そして‥‥華宮　霧緒が 罪に問われることになるでしょう。
English: And in his place, Adrian Andrews would be charged with murder.

Japanese: （き、霧緒さんが‥‥殺人罪に！）
English: (M-Ms. Andrews... would be charged with murder!)

Japanese: 検察側の質問は終了しました。 最後に、弁護側の意見を聞きます。
English: The prosecution has no further questions, so we will now hear the defense's final remarks.

Japanese: 係官！　被告人・王都楼　真悟を 証言台へ‥‥！
English: Bailiff! Please bring the defendant, Matt Engarde, to the stand!

Japanese: ‥‥犯人の遺留品は 間に合わなかった‥‥。
English: The items from De Killer's hideout didn't make it in time...

Japanese: もう‥‥ 時間切れ、ね‥‥
English: We tried as hard as we could, but it looks like our time has run out...
(I can't believe it...)

Japanese: あなたのヒトコトで‥‥ 判決は決まるわ！
English: The outcome now lies in your hands.

Japanese: やれやれ‥‥やっと 判決ですか。
English: Dude, did the old guy finally decide?

Japanese: 正直なところ、あなたが 潔白だとは考えられません。
English: To be honest, I can't think of you as a truly innocent and good person.

Japanese: ‥‥ひとりの女性を、自殺にまで、 追いこみました。
English: You have done enough evil to drive a woman to suicide.

Japanese: しかし‥‥少なくとも、
English: But...

Japanese: 殺人の罪に関しては 無実だったようですね。
English: At least on the charge of murder, it would appear you are innocent.

Japanese: ハッ‥‥！
English: Hah...!

Japanese: やっとわかったかよ‥‥ このボンクラどもが！
English: So, I guess even the old fuddy-duddy figured me out!

Japanese: ひ、被告人‥‥？
English: M-Mr. Engarde...?

Japanese: ひでェ弁護士だったぜ。 ‥‥ここまで暴かれちゃア、
English: You were atrocious as a lawyer, weren't you? Giving your client away like this!

Japanese: “春風のようにサワヤカ” なんて、お笑いグサだもんな！
English: And that "refreshing like a spring breeze" crap; it's just as atrocious, don't you agree?

Japanese: ‥‥とにかくさァ、早いトコ、 無罪判決をいただこうか。
English: Anyway, get on with it and pronounce me innocent already.

Japanese: なァ、弁護士さんよォ！
English: Right, Mister Lawyer!?

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんの命を取るか‥‥ 正義を取るか‥‥？）
English: (Should I side with justice? Or should I save Maya's life?)

Japanese: 王都楼のコト‥‥ かならず有罪にしてやってよね！
English: You'd better get Engarde a guilty sentence, OK!?

Japanese: （そんなことをしたら‥‥ ‥‥きみは、死ぬんだぞ！）
English: (But if I did that... Maya will die!)

Japanese: （だからと言って‥‥王都楼を 無罪にしてしまったら‥‥）
English: (But if I say he's innocent...)

Japanese: （‥‥彼女が、殺人者として 裁かれてしまう！）
English: (Then Ms. Andrews will be charged as the murderer!)

Japanese: （‥‥有罪か、無罪か‥‥）
English: (Do I say he's "guilty"... or "not guilty"...?)

Japanese: （どっちを選んでも‥‥ だれかの人生が、終わる！）
English: (Either choice I make, someone's life is going to end!)

Japanese: （ぼくの“選択”によって‥‥！）
English: (It all hinges on what I "choose"!)

Japanese: ‥‥では、弁護人。
English: Now then, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: あなたの、最終的な 結論を聞かせてもらいましょう。
English: Let's hear the defense's final statements on this matter.

Japanese: 殺し屋に殺人を依頼したのが 華宮　霧緒ならば‥‥
English: If the person who hired the assassin was Adrian Andrews...

Japanese: あなたの依頼人 王都楼　真悟は、無罪なのです。
English: Then your client, Mr. Matt Engarde is innocent.

Japanese: ま‥‥聞くまでも ないケドなァ‥‥
English: Hmph... There's no need to ask, old man.

Japanese: だって、このオレの 弁護士なんだからさ‥‥
English: After all, my lawyer is going to say what I want... aren't you?

Japanese: な‥‥成歩堂‥‥
English: Wright...

Japanese: （ダメだ‥‥！ 答えるしかない‥‥）
English: (I can't! I can't do this! But I have to decide something!)

Japanese: （もう、証拠はアテにならない。 心の声にしたがうんだ！）
English: (I can't count on the evidence to help me anymore. I have to listen to my heart!)

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくの依頼人は‥‥ 王都楼　真悟は‥‥
English: My client... Matt Engarde is...

Japanese: どうして答えないのですか！ 成歩堂くん！
English: We are waiting for your answer, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟は‥‥ あなたの依頼人なのですぞ！
English: Matt Engarde, your client deserves an answer!

Japanese: （‥‥ごめん‥‥真宵ちゃん）
English: (Maya... I'm sorry...)

Japanese: ‥‥王都楼　真悟は‥‥
English: Matt Engarde is...
F-Franziska von Karma!!

Japanese: ど、どうして あなたが、ここに‥‥
English: Wh-What are you doing here...

Japanese: かぶッ！
English: OWW!

Japanese: これでわかったでしょう？ 成歩堂　龍一‥‥
English: You see now, don't you... Mr. Phoenix Wright?

Japanese: あのヒゲから、 目をはなしてはイケナイ、と！
English: This is exactly why you should NEVER take your eyes off of that scruffy fool!

Japanese: ‥‥持ってきたのか‥‥？ 最後の証拠品を‥‥
English: Did you bring them? The final pieces... Do you have them?

Japanese: 知ってるはずよ、御剣　怜侍。
English: You should know better than to ask that, Mr. Miles Edgeworth.

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔は、すべて カンペキをもってヨシとする！
English: A Von Karma is perfect in every way! The evidence is here in perfect condition!

Japanese: 安心しなさい。 ヒゲのケガは軽い‥‥
English: Don't worry about Scruffy. He's fine, and his injuries are minor.

Japanese: 遺留品は、コイツにつつんで 持ってきたわ。
English: All of the items are inside this.

Japanese: ずいぶんとまあ、うすぎたない コートですねえ‥‥
English: What a filthy, old coat this is...

Japanese: （‥‥イトノコさんのだな。 あのフクザツな色あいは‥‥）
English: (That's Gumshoe's... I can spot his tattered rags anywhere...)

Japanese: 見苦しい点は、おわびするわ。 他につつむものがなかったから。
English: I apologize for its ugliness, but there was nothing else to wrap the items in.

Japanese: （あの、うすぎたない コートの中身のために‥‥）
English: (I've fought long and hard this whole trial...)

Japanese: （ぼくは、今まで 持ちこたえてきたんだ！）
English: (All for what is inside that raggedy coat...)

Japanese: （きっと‥‥あのコートが、 決定的な手がかりをくれる！）
English: (I'm sure that inside that coat, lies a crucial piece of evidence!)

Japanese: 裁判長！　そのうすぎたない コートの中には‥‥
English: Your Honor! Inside that filthy coat...

Japanese: 弁護側の、最後の証拠品が 入っているのです！
English: Are the defense's final pieces of evidence!

Japanese: ‥‥最後の‥‥証拠品ですとォ！
English: Your final... evidence!?

Japanese: この審理はすでに、判決を 残すのみとなっています。
English: This trial is already over. All that remains is for me to hand down my verdict.

Japanese: 今さら、どんな証拠が 提出されようと‥‥
English: I do not believe that any evidence presented now,

Japanese: それをくつがえすことは、 不可能と考えます。
English: would change the outcome of this trial.

Japanese: （な‥‥なんだって‥‥！）
English: (Wh-What!?)

Japanese: 証拠を最後の１つまで 調べるのは、われわれのギムだ。
English: Your Honor. It is our duty to examine every piece of evidence, down to the last.

Japanese: 狩魔検事より、 証拠品の提出を要請する！
English: I request that Ms. von Karma be allowed to present these pieces of evidence!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: しかたありませんな‥‥ 検事。提示を許可しましょう。
English: I suppose you are right, Mr. Edgeworth. I grant permission to do so.

Japanese: しかし‥‥当然のことながら。
English: However, this one obvious rule applies here;

Japanese: 新しい事実が浮かび上がらない かぎり、証拠は受理しません。
English: If these items do not bring up any new points, then they will not be accepted by this court.

Japanese: ‥‥では、狩魔検事。 おねがいします。
English: Now, Ms. von Karma. If you please.

Japanese: ‥‥これらの証拠品は、虎狼死家が 逃走の際、残していったものよ。
English: These pieces of evidence are items left by De Killer during his escape from the police.

Japanese: 遺留品、というワケですか‥‥
English: Hmm... He must have been in quite a rush.

Japanese: そういうコト。‥‥ヤツは、 ３つの証拠を残しているわ。
English: Yes, Your Honor. De Killer left three pieces of evidence.

Japanese: （‥‥これから提示される 証拠品の中に‥‥）
English: (Somewhere among the evidence we're about to see...)

Japanese: （この、絶望的な状況を一気に 打開する“奇跡”が‥‥）
English: (There will be something that will turn this whole situation around... like a miracle.)

Japanese: （きっと、 かくされているハズだ！）
English: (I'm sure of it!)

Japanese: それが‥‥私たちの、 最後の希望よ‥‥。
English: That is all we can hope for.

Japanese: 遺留品の１つ目は ‥‥ピストルね。
English: The first item is a pistol.

Japanese: （虎狼死家のピストルか。 事件に関係あるのか‥‥？）
English: (Does De Killer's pistol have anything to do with this case...?)

Japanese: そのピストル‥‥何か、 今回の事件に関係が？
English: Does that pistol have any relation to this case?

Japanese: 弾道のテストをしていないから なんとも言えないけど‥‥
English: We have yet to perform a ballistics test, so I can't say anything for certain...

Japanese: 関係はあると思うわ。 ‥‥少なくとも、私には。
English: However, I believe it has something to do with this case... At least, to me.

Japanese: キミを狙撃したピストル ‥‥というワケか。
English: That's the pistol that he used to shoot you, isn't it?

Japanese: おそらく、ね。
English: That's what I believe, yes.
Oh...

Japanese: 肩から摘出された弾丸なら、 記念にとってあるから‥‥
English: I kept the bullet they removed from my shoulder as a sort of memento...

Japanese: いいサンプルに なるわね、きっと。
English: I'm sure it will be an excellent sample for the test.

Japanese: （狩魔　冥を狙撃した ピストル、か‥‥）
English: (So that's the pistol that was used to shoot Franziska...)

Japanese: （何かの手がかりに なるかもしれないな‥‥）
English: (It's probably not going to help us very much...)

Japanese: 証拠品《ピストル》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Pistol added to the Court Record.

Japanese: （今回の事件に、ピストルは 使われていないはずだ）
English: (I'm pretty sure there was no gun involved in this case.)

Japanese: （そんな証拠のハナシを聞いても しかたがないな‥‥）
English: (There's no real benefit to hearing about it...)

Japanese: ‥‥次の証拠品を 提示してください。
English: Please present the next piece of evidence.

Japanese: 証拠品《ピストル》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Pistol added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ２つ目の遺留品よ。 ‥‥これは、ビデオテープね。
English: The second piece of evidence is this video tape.

Japanese: （おそらく‥‥王都楼の屋敷から 持ち出されたものだろう‥‥）
English: (I bet De Killer took that from Engarde Mansion...)

Japanese: そのテープ‥‥内容は カクニンしましたか？
English: Have you checked the contents of that tape?

Japanese: ザンネンながら、 そんな時間はなかったわ。
English: Unfortunately, there was no time to.

Japanese: ‥‥ううう‥‥
English: Oh yeah...

Japanese: ただ‥‥このテープ、 大切なものみたいね。
English: But I would speculate that this tape is very important.

Japanese: どうしてですか？
English: Why would you say that?

Japanese: 取りもどしに来たから。 ‥‥現場に。
English: Because he came back to his hideout for it.

Japanese: こ、虎狼死家が‥‥？
English: De... De Killer went back for it...?

Japanese: そう。このテープを 奪いかえそうとして。
English: That's right. It looks like he was trying to recover it.

Japanese: その場にいた 警官３名が、負傷したわ。
English: He injured three of the officers at the site.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 彼ら、なんとか守りぬいた みたいだけど‥‥
English: But somehow, it looks like they managed to protect it from De Killer.

Japanese: とんでもない男よ、 虎狼死家　左々右エ門は。
English: Shelly de Killer is no ordinary man.

Japanese: 証拠品《ビデオテープ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Video Tape added to the Court Record.

Japanese: （今さら、虎狼死家の犯行を 立証してもしょうがない‥‥）
English: (Right now, we don't need to establish De Killer's role in this case...)

Japanese: （他の証拠のハナシを聞こう）
English: (I should be listening to information about a different piece of evidence.)

Japanese: ‥‥それは、もういいです。 次の証拠品を！
English: That's fine. Please present the next piece of evidence!

Japanese: 証拠品《ビデオテープ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Video Tape added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 次が最後よ。 ‥‥これは、ボーイの制服ね。
English: The last piece of evidence is this bellboy's uniform.

Japanese: （あれは‥‥ホテル・バンドーで 見た制服か‥‥）
English: (Is that a uniform from the Gatewater Hotel...?)

Japanese: それは‥‥犯行に 使われたんですか？
English: Was that used during the crime?

Japanese: ほぼ、まちがいなく。
English: I am almost certain it was.

Japanese: ポケットには、黒い 革の手袋も入っていたわ。
English: There's even a pair of black leather gloves in one of the pockets.

Japanese: （まちがいない！　あの夜、 あいつが着ていた制服だ！）
English: (There's no doubt about it! De Killer was wearing this on the night of the murder!)

Japanese: それに‥‥１つ、 おもしろいことがあるの。
English: There is one thing I found interesting about this uniform.

Japanese: なんだろうか。
English: And what is that?

Japanese: この制服‥‥ ボタンが１つ、取れてるわ。
English: There is a button missing on this uniform.

Japanese: ボタンが‥‥？
English: A button...?

Japanese: かなり特殊なボタンよ。 落ちているところを探せば‥‥
English: It's a very unique button. I'm sure if we were to recover it...

Japanese: おもしろい手がかりに なりそうね。
English: It would provide us with an interesting clue.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 証拠品《ボーイの制服》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Bellboy's Uniform added to the Court Record.

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら、コイツは 手がかりになりそうもないな）
English: (I don't think this is going to help us much at all...)

Japanese: どうもありがとう、狩魔検事。 もう、じゅうぶんです。
English: Thank you very much, Ms. von Karma. I think that's enough.

Japanese: ‥‥フッ。
English: ...Hmph.

Japanese: 証拠品《ボーイの制服》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Bellboy's Uniform added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥遺留品の提示は、 以上で終わりよ。
English: That is all I have to present, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: ‥‥やはり、 思ったとおりでしたな。
English: It's just as I thought.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことですか？
English: And... what is that, Your Honor...?

Japanese: 虎狼死家　左々右エ門が アジトに残した、遺留品‥‥
English: I'm sure, were we under normal circumstances,

Japanese: たしかに、ふつうならば 重要な手がかりになるでしょう。
English: these items from Shelly de Killer's hideout would be very important clues.

Japanese: しかし‥‥
English: However...

Japanese: われわれにとって問題なのは、 “犯人がだれか”ではありません。
English: Our question is not "Who did the killing?"

Japanese: ‥‥“依頼人が、だれか”‥‥
English: It is, "Who is the client?"...

Japanese: そのとおりです。
English: Yes, that is correct.

Japanese: ‥‥これら３つの遺留品は、 その事実を物語るものではない！
English: And these three items do not tell us anything about that!

Japanese: ‥‥ごくろうさま、狩魔検事。 下がってけっこうです。
English: Thank you for your hard work, Ms. von Karma. You may step down now.

Japanese: 待ってください！
English: Wait, Your Honor!

Japanese: これらの証拠品に関して、 尋問をさせてください！
English: Please allow me to examine this new evidence!

Japanese: ‥‥却下します。
English: Overruled.

Japanese: 本法廷にはすでに、判決に 必要な証拠はそろっています！
English: This court already has all the evidence it needs to hand down a verdict!

Japanese: ‥‥めでたしめでたし、 ってヤツだな？　センセイ‥‥
English: Wonderful... Absolutely splendid. This judge is such a brilliant man, isn't he?

Japanese: （‥‥ダメ、なのか？）
English: (Is this the end...?)

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix.

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥起こらないから “奇跡”なんでしょうか‥‥
English: I knew it... There's no such thing as a "miracle" in this world, is there?

Japanese: ‥‥それはちがうわ。
English: I think you're wrong. I think they do exist.

Japanese: 起こすからこそ“奇跡”なのよ。
English: But you have to make that "miracle" happen.

Japanese: ‥‥ここまで来て、 あきらめてはダメ！
English: You've come this far! You can't give up now!

Japanese: で、でも‥‥！ どう考えたって、もう‥‥
English: But... But... No matter how you think about it... It's... It's...

Japanese: 考えてみて。
English: Try... For my sake. Just think about it for a second.

Japanese: ‥‥この状況を切りぬけるには、 ２つの方法が残されているわ。
English: There are two ways out of this situation for us.

Japanese: （ふ、ふたつも‥‥！）
English: (T-Two!?)
The first...

Japanese: “王都楼が、ココロの底から 有罪判決を望むこと”
English: Make Engarde wish from the bottom of his soul for a guilty verdict.
Huh?

Japanese: 虎狼死家は、依頼人の希望を 最優先するわ。
English: De Killer will always place his client's wishes first.

Japanese: 王都楼が有罪を望めば‥‥ 人質を解放するでしょう。
English: If Engarde himself wishes to be convicted, then he will let his hostage go.

Japanese: そ、そりゃそうですけど‥‥
English: Th-That may be true, but...

Japanese: そんなコト、 あり得ないじゃないですか！
English: That's asking me to do the impossible!
The second way...

Japanese: “虎狼死家が、一方的に 王都楼との契約を終了すること”
English: Force De Killer to end his contract with Engarde.

Japanese: 虎狼死家が王都楼を“依頼人”と 考えるのをやめれば‥‥
English: If De Killer were to no longer think of Engarde as his "client"...

Japanese: 彼は真宵を解放するわ。
English: Then he would let Maya go.

Japanese: ‥‥千尋さん！ そんなの、もっとあり得ない！
English: Mia! That's even more impossible!

Japanese: 依頼人に対する“仁義”を、 何よりも大切にする男なんですよ！
English: He is a man who values his duty towards his clients above all else!

Japanese: たしかに、２つとも 一見、あり得ない状況よ。
English: I know both of these seem like impossible feats at first.

Japanese: だからこそ、“奇跡”なんだから。
English: But if you could make either one happen, it would truly be a "miracle".

Japanese: それに‥‥！
English: The bigger problem is,

Japanese: あの裁判長はもう、証拠品を 必要としていません！
English: the judge has already said he doesn't need any more evidence!

Japanese: 今さら証拠品なんか、 受けとってくれませんよ！
English: The pieces he was just shown; he's not accepting them!

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix.

Japanese: いつも、そうだったでしょう？ ‥‥発想を逆転させるの。
English: Think things through from the other side. Isn't that what has always worked for us?

Japanese: （い‥‥今になって このコトバが‥‥）
English: (The "other side"...? Wait, does she mean...)

Japanese: ‥‥逆転‥‥ですか‥‥？
English: You mean... to turn things around?

Japanese: 裁判長は、証拠品を 必要としていない。
English: Phoenix. The judge says he doesn't need the evidence.

Japanese: ‥‥それならば‥‥証拠品を 必要としているのは、だれ？
English: If that's the case, then who does need it?

Japanese: （“証拠品を 必要としている人物”‥‥）
English: ("The person who needs the evidence"...)

Japanese: 弁護士、検事、裁判長‥‥ 彼らは、すべての証拠を見ている。
English: The defense, prosecution, and the judge... We have seen all the pieces of evidence.

Japanese: だから、“真実”を 知ることができるのよ。
English: And that is how we have come to know the "truth".

Japanese: でも‥‥そうでない 人たちだっているわ。
English: But there are people who have not seen them all.

Japanese: 彼らは、“真実”を知らない。
English: And those people do not know the "truth".

Japanese: その“真実”が‥‥ 最後の奇跡を起こすかもしれない。
English: That truth... It may be what will bring about the miracle in the end.

Japanese: ‥‥今度こそ、 異議はないようですね。
English: There are no objections this time, correct?

Japanese: では、王都楼　真悟に、 判決を言いわたします！
English: Now then, I will pronounce my verdict!

Japanese: ‥‥つつしんで、 うかがいましょうか‥‥。
English: Why don't we all respectfully sit back and listen, kids.

Japanese: ‥‥言ったはずです、 成歩堂くん。
English: I have already told you, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 本法廷は、もう 証拠品は必要ない、と。
English: This court does not need any more evidence.

Japanese: 証拠を必要としているのは、 ぼくたちだけではない！
English: I am not saying it is us that needs the evidence, Your Honor!

Japanese: ‥‥その人物に‥‥ 証拠を提示したい、と‥‥？
English: Then... you want to show the evidence to... that person...?

Japanese: そうです。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: おねがいします！
English: Please, Your Honor!

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂くん。 そこまで言うのならば‥‥
English: Mr. Wright. For you to ask with such passion...

Japanese: １回だけ、許可しましょう。
English: I will grant you one chance.
(O...One chance...)

Japanese: あなたが正しいと思う人物に、 その証拠を提示してください。
English: Please show your evidence to who you think is the right person.

Japanese: ‥‥不可能だ！　たった１回で、 この状況を逆転するなど‥‥
English: That's impossible! To turn this situation around in one try...

Japanese: ‥‥一度だけです。
English: One try. That is all I will permit.

Japanese: （‥‥思い出すんだ‥‥）
English: (I have to try to remember...)

Japanese: （ぼくがたどってきた、 すべての物語を‥‥）
English: (Everything that has happened up to this point...)

Japanese: （そして、考えろ！）
English: (Think, Phoenix! Think!)

Japanese: （‥‥真宵ちゃんを助けて、 同時に王都楼を倒す方法を！）
English: (There must be a way to save Maya while taking Engarde down at the same time!)

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。 まず、うかがいましょう。
English: Now then, Mr. Wright. Let's not waste any more time.

Japanese: どなたに証拠品を つきつけるつもりですか？
English: Who would you like to show evidence to?

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: I see.

Japanese: それでは、つづけて 提示していただきましょう。
English: And now...

Japanese: その人物に つきつけるべき証拠品を！
English: Tell this court what one piece of evidence you would like to show this person!

Japanese: いかがですか？ ‥‥御剣検事。
English: Well, what do you think, Mr. Edgeworth...?

Japanese: う‥‥うム‥‥ 私には、なんとも‥‥
English: Uh... Um... I... don't have anything to say to this...

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ 狩魔検事は？
English: Hmm... How about you, Ms. von Karma?

Japanese: ‥‥私はその‥‥ちょっと、 狙撃されたばかりだから‥‥
English: Well, I was just shot, so...

Japanese: 弁護人の主張は これ以上、聞くことはできません。
English: I'm afraid I cannot allow the defense to continue.

Japanese: ええええッ！
English: Whaaat!?

Japanese: ‥‥あなた以外、ダレにも イミがわからないようですからな！
English: No one understands what you are talking about anymore!

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと待ってください！ もう一度‥‥
English: W-Wait! Please! One more chance...

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、今度こそ‥‥
English: That is enough, Mr. Wright. I will now state my verdict.

Japanese: 王都楼　真悟に 判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Matt Engarde...

Japanese: ‥‥では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. This court is adjourned!

Japanese: ‥‥こうして、 事件は終わった。
English: And just like that, the case came to an end.

Japanese: ぼくは逃げるように 法廷を‥‥街をあとにした。
English: I ran away from the courtroom... and wandered the streets alone.

Japanese: あれ以来、真宵ちゃんには 会っていない。
English: I never saw Maya again.

Japanese: 虎狼死家はヤクソクをまもる。 きっと、解放されたはずだ。
English: De Killer is a man of his word, so I'm sure he released her as promised.

Japanese: 数日後‥‥霧緒さんの 審理のニュースを聞いた。
English: I heard the verdict of Ms. Andrews' trial a few days later.

Japanese: もちろん、判決は《有罪》だった。
English: She was found guilty, of course.

Japanese: ‥‥“奇跡”は起こらなかった。
English: The "miracle" never happened.

Japanese: 当然なのかもしれない。
English: Maybe it was never meant to.

Japanese: 起こらないからこそ “奇跡”なのだから‥‥。
English: Because a "miracle" is something that doesn't exist.

Japanese: おわり
English: THE END

Japanese: いかがですか？ ‥‥御剣検事。
English: Well, what do you think, Mr. Edgeworth...?

Japanese: う‥‥うム‥‥
English: Uh... Um...

Japanese: ‥‥見せてみる価値は ある‥‥そう判断する。
English: I think there is some merit... in showing this evidence to that witness.

Japanese: 係官！　さきほどの無線機を！
English: Bailiff! Please bring in the transceiver from earlier!

Japanese: （‥‥よし！ なんとか、つながった‥‥）
English: (Alright! It looks like they got a hold of him...)

Japanese: ‥‥真宵ちゃんは‥‥ ブジなんだろうな？
English: Maya... She's OK, right!?

Japanese: それより、早く決着を つけようではありませんか。
English: Didn't I tell you to concern yourself with bringing about a speedy end to this trial?

Japanese: お話では、証拠を１つ、 見ればいいというコトでしたか。
English: Now, if I understand correctly, you wish to show me one piece of evidence?

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおり。
English: Yes. One is all I need.

Japanese: ここに、ビデオテープがあります。 あなたのアジトで発見した。
English: I have here a video tape. It was found at your hideout.

Japanese: これを取りもどそうとして、 ３人の警官にケガをさせた、とか。
English: I heard you injured three officers in your attempt to get this back.

Japanese: ‥‥彼らには、 もうしわけないことを。
English: That was most regrettable.

Japanese: 依頼人から、たのまれて いたのでございます。
English: However, it was an order from my client.

Japanese: なんとしても、そのテープを 守りぬいてほしい、と。
English: I was told to protect that video tape.

Japanese: （やっぱり、そうだったか‥‥）
English: (I thought so...)

Japanese: ザンネンながら‥‥ ミスをしてしまいましたが。
English: I'm afraid I seem to have failed in that regard.

Japanese: テープの中身を、あなたは 知っていますか？
English: Do you know the contents of this tape?

Japanese: ‥‥依頼人から“再生するな”と カタく言われてましたから。
English: I was sternly told by my client to not watch it.

Japanese: まったく、存じません。
English: So I have absolutely no idea.

Japanese: ‥‥コイツには、あなたの姿が 記録されています。
English: Actually, you are on this tape.

Japanese: 私が‥‥？
English: Me?

Japanese: あの殺人現場には、 ビデオカメラがしかけられていた。
English: There was a video camera hidden at the crime scene.

Japanese: あなたの犯行は‥‥ 録画されていたんですよ。
English: Your actions were being recorded.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですと！
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: ほ‥‥ホントですか！ 成歩堂くん！
English: Is that true!? Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: いったい‥‥ダレが カメラなど‥‥！
English: Who... Who was it that planted a camera...!?

Japanese: あらかじめ、現場にカメラを しかけることができたのは‥‥
English: Well, the only person who could have placed a camera at the scene of the crime...

Japanese: あなたの“依頼人”しか いません。
English: would be your "client" naturally.

Japanese: だ、だからそれは‥‥ 華宮　霧緒‥‥
English: Th-That was... Adrian Andrews...

Japanese: ダマって聞くことだ‥‥裁判長。
English: Be quiet and listen... Your Honor.

Japanese: は。
English: Yes, sir.

Japanese: ‥‥“依頼人”は、犯行の日時を あなたに指定したそうですね。
English: Your "client" specified a place and time for you, isn't that right?

Japanese: は、はい‥‥たしかに。
English: Y-Yes...

Japanese: このビデオを撮影する ためだったんですよ。
English: That was so they could film you.

Japanese: 知りませんでした。
English: I had no idea.

Japanese: 成歩堂さん。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 私の依頼人は、なぜ そんなコトをしたのですか？
English: Why would my client do such a thing?

Japanese: その理由が‥‥ どうしても知りたいのですが。
English: I would like to know why...

Japanese: （王都楼　真悟が殺害現場を 撮影した理由‥‥）
English: (Why did Matt Engarde film the crime scene...?)

Japanese: （‥‥それこそが、この究極の ピンチを切りぬけるカギだ！）
English: (The reason "why" he did that is my ticket out of this whole mess!)

Japanese: あなたの“依頼人”が犯行を 盗撮した理由は、ただ１つ。
English: There is only one reason why your "client" would secretly film the crime scene. They...

Japanese: 藤見野　イサオが殺害される ところを見たかったからです！
English: They wanted to witness the moment Juan Corrida died!

Japanese: おそらく、ふかふかの ソファーにすわって‥‥
English: Your client was going to sit on their fluffy soft sofa...

Japanese: コーヒーでも飲みながら上映会、 とシャレこむつもりだった！
English: Sip some coffee and pretend they were at a film festival!

Japanese: そ‥‥それは‥‥
English: That... That's...

Japanese: あまりシュミがよいとは 思えませんね‥‥。
English: I don't think that sounds like a very enjoyable hobby...

Japanese: どうですか‥‥裁判長！
English: Well, Your Honor!?

Japanese: “依頼人”は、あなたのウデを 信用していなかったのです！
English: Your "client" didn't trust your skills!

Japanese: だから、シゴトぶりを監視する ために、カメラをしかけた！
English: That's why, in order to monitor your work, they set up a video camera!

Japanese: そ、そうだったのですか。
English: I-Is that right?

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、私のシゴトが シッパイすれば‥‥
English: I suppose that were I to fail at my job...

Japanese: 依頼人にとっては、 身のハメツですからな。
English: my client would fall into utter ruin.

Japanese: シゴトぶりを見たい、というキモチ は、わからないでもありません。
English: I can't say I don't understand the want to see how I do my job.

Japanese: キチンと消去していただけるのなら ‥‥大目に見ることにしましょう。
English: And as long as I could cleanly erase the tape... I suppose it can be overlooked.

Japanese: あれ‥‥そうですか。
English: Oh... Is that how you feel...?

Japanese: み、みなさんは‥‥ どう思いますか？
English: W-Well, everyone...? What do you think...? Pretty good, huh...?

Japanese: 以前、あなたの“依頼人”は‥‥
English: Your "client" once told me something very interesting.

Japanese: あなたについて、 こんなことを言っていました。
English: We were talking about you, and this is what they said.

Japanese: オレは、だれも信じない。 ‥‥特に、殺し屋なんて人種はな。
English: But I'm no weakling. I don't believe anyone... least of all assassins.

Japanese: “殺し屋”から“ゆすり屋”に 転職を考えるかもしれないだろ？
English: Oh, come now, Mr. Wright. Assassins aren't above blackmail.

Japanese: そこで、あのビデオが モノを言う、ってワケだ。
English: Yes, that's where the video comes in.

Japanese: こっちが逆に、ヤツを 脅迫することだってできる。
English: With that, I can keep him at bay, and even blackmail him if I want.

Japanese: “依頼人”は、あなたを まったく信用していなかった。
English: Your "client" didn't trust you at all.

Japanese: このビデオを使って、あなたを 脅迫することまで考えていた。
English: They were thinking of using this video to blackmail you!

Japanese: いかがですか！ 虎狼死家　左々右エ門！
English: What do you have to say to that, Shelly de Killer!?

Japanese: ぐ‥‥ぐぐ‥‥
English: Gnnn...nnnngh...

Japanese: おほオォォォォッ！
English: Ohoooooo!

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、私は‥‥
English: It looks like...

Japanese: 最初から、ウラ切られて いたようですね‥‥。
English: It looks like I was being deceived from the very beginning...

Japanese: ええ。ミゴトに‥‥
English: Yes... By a natural...

Japanese: そういうヤツだったんですよ。
English: That is the kind of person they are.

Japanese: 自分自身は、いっさいのキケンを おかすことなく、
English: Your "client" is a person who only thinks and plots of how to use the people around them

Japanese: まわりの人間を利用する ことしか、考えていない‥‥
English: to protect themselves from any and all dangers that may arise.

Japanese: それがあなたの“依頼人”の 正体だったのです。
English: That is the true nature of your "client".

Japanese: ‥‥そこで、１つだけ 聞きたいことがある。
English: I have one question for the witness.

Japanese: なんでしょう。
English: Yes?

Japanese: 証人はわれわれに、 何度も、こう発言している。
English: You told us one thing numerous times during your testimony.

Japanese: “ウラ切り者を、最もニクむ” ‥‥と。
English: You said that you detest traitors most of all.

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおりです。
English: Yes, that's right.

Japanese: ではもし、あなたの依頼人が ウラ切り者だとしたら‥‥
English: But what if that traitor was your own client...?

Japanese: どうするつもりかッ！
English: What would you do then!?

Japanese: もちろん‥‥
English: That's obvious.

Japanese: ‥‥契約は、その場で 打ち切らせていただきます。
English: I would break our contract in that case.

Japanese: そして‥‥
English: And then...

Japanese: その依頼人こそが、私の 次のターゲットになるでしょう。
English: That client would become my next target.

Japanese: 虎狼死家の名にかけて、 この命にかえても‥‥
English: For the honor of the De Killer name, even if it takes an eternity...

Japanese: ‥‥追いつめて ごらんにいれましょう。
English: I would follow that person to the ends of the earth to exact my punishment.

Japanese: ‥‥なるほど。
English: I see.

Japanese: そのヒトコトが聞きたかった‥‥
English: That's all I wanted to know.

Japanese: （ウラ切り者が、次の ターゲットに‥‥）
English: (So the traitor becomes De Killer's next target...)

Japanese: （そうか‥‥）
English: (Ah! I get it...)

Japanese: （これが、ただ１つの‥‥ “逆転”へ通じる道だったんだ！）
English: (This is how we'll turn this case around!)

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂さま。
English: Mr. Wright.
Yes...?

Japanese: ただ今をもって、依頼人との 契約を終了します。
English: My contract with my client is over as of now.

Japanese: ‥‥新しいシゴトが できましたのでね。
English: I seem to have a new job on my hands.

Japanese: あなたのおニモツは‥‥ セキニンを持って、お返しします。
English: I will now return to you, your precious item.

Japanese: ‥‥ナニよ！　おニモツって！
English: ...What the!? I'm not an item!

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら‥‥ 助かったみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Maya... I thought I'd never see you again...)
(Oh thank goodness!)

Japanese: ‥‥ええと、どうやら審理は １つの結論に達したようですが‥‥
English: Um... This trial appears to have come to its conclusion...

Japanese: しかし、その‥‥じつは私には、 ナニがなんだか‥‥
English: However... I... Actually, I am sort of...

Japanese: ケッキョクその、 依頼人は‥‥どなただったので
English: I don't quite know what just happened there with the client and the witness and...

Japanese: はがッ！
English: Gwaaah!

Japanese: か、狩魔検事‥‥！ いったい、どこからッ！
English: M-Ms. von Karma! Where did that...!?

Japanese: ‥‥彼女はいつも、 あなたをねらっている。
English: She always has you in her sights.

Japanese: さあ！　今度こそ、 判決をいただこうか！
English: Now! I do believe it's time to finally hand down a verdict!

Japanese: 王都楼さん。
English: Mr. Engarde.

Japanese: どうやら、あなたの お望みどおりになりましたね。
English: It looks like somehow, you got what you wanted.

Japanese: ‥‥あなたは、ついに 無罪判決を受けるわけです。
English: You will finally receive the acquittal you wanted so badly. You should be happy.

Japanese: しかし‥‥１つだけ言っておく。
English: But before that, I would like to make one final statement.

Japanese: 近い将来、ウデキキの殺し屋が 被告の前にあらわれるだろう。
English: Sometime in the near future, one very betrayed assassin may appear before you.

Japanese: その男のシゴトは、すばやく、 そして、たしかだ。
English: Needless to say, that man is very good at what he does.

Japanese: そのようすは、このビデオを 見れば、よくわかるだろう。
English: I'm sure you would understand what I mean, if you watch this video.

Japanese: ‥‥助けて‥‥
English: Help me...

Japanese: では、裁判長！ 判決をおねがいしよう。
English: Now then, Your Honor! The verdict, if you please.

Japanese: よ‥‥よろしいですかな？ 弁護人。
English: I-Is this alright with you, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥すでに勝負はついたわ。
English: We have finally reached the end of a very long battle.

Japanese: 無罪にせよ、有罪にせよ‥‥ 彼に逃げ道はない。
English: Whether he's convicted or acquitted, there is no escape for him now.

Japanese: ‥‥さあ。 好きに料理してあげなさい。
English: Go on, Phoenix. Plead whichever way your heart tells you.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: Right, Chief.

Japanese: ‥‥おめでとうございます、 王都楼　真悟さん。
English: Congratulations, Mr. Matt Engarde.

Japanese: せいぜい楽しんでください。 ‥‥残り少ない人生を。
English: Please make sure to savor every moment of what little time you have left.

Japanese: 裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護側は、今度こそ 無罪判決を要求します。
English: As always, the defense pleads not guilty.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: それでは、王都楼　真悟に 判決を言いわたします。
English: This court finds the defendant, Matt Engarde...

Japanese: ま‥‥待ってくれ‥‥
English: P-Please wait...

Japanese: ど、どうしましたか？
English: What's the matter?

Japanese: 無罪判決を受けたら‥‥ こ‥‥殺される‥‥
English: If... If I get a not guilty... I'll... I'll be... killed...

Japanese: ‥‥王都楼　真悟。
English: ...Matt Engarde.

Japanese: たしかにぼくは弁護士だ。 だけど‥‥
English: Even though I am a lawyer,

Japanese: 殺人の罪を、ゼロにする ことは、ぼくにはできない。
English: I cannot make your crime disappear.

Japanese: ‥‥“有罪”を主張したほうが いいと思うよ。
English: I think a guilty verdict is appropriate here.

Japanese: こ‥‥このオレが‥‥ ゆ、ゆうざい‥‥
English: M-Me!? My wonderful self...!? G-Guilty!?

Japanese: もし、無罪判決を受けて 留置所を出てしまったら‥‥
English: Even if you got an acquittal, the instant you set foot out- side the detention center,

Japanese: きみの生命は、いきなり 究極のキケンにさらされる。
English: your life would be in danger.

Japanese: ‥‥いずれにせよ‥‥
English: No matter which way you look at it...

Japanese: きみはもう、 ゼッタイに逃げられない！
English: You can't run away from your crime anymore!

Japanese: うおおおおおおおおおおおおおおお おおおおおおおおおおおおおおおお
English: Noooooooooooooooo oooooooooooooooooo oooooooooooooooooo!

Japanese: おおおお有オオ罪おオオヲ有ヲお罪 ヲヲヲヲおおヲヲオ有オおお罪ォ有
English: Guiltyyyyyyy!!!!!! !!!!!!!!Guuuuuilty!!! GGG--Guuiiillltyyyy!!!

Japanese: ォォォ有ォォ罪ォォォォォ有罪ォォ ォ有罪ォォォ有罪ォ有罪ゥ有ゥ罪ッ
English: GGGUUUILLLTYYY!!GUILTY!!! GuiLTy!!guIIIltYY!!Guil-- guilty!guilty!GUilTY!GUILTY!

Japanese: ‥‥今度こそ、私たちは 真実にたどりついたようですね。
English: As always, it looks like we have uncovered the real truth.

Japanese: （あなた以外はね。裁判長‥‥）
English: (We? I don't remember you helping out much in this...)

Japanese: ‥‥御剣検事。 王都楼　真悟は‥‥？
English: Mr. Edgeworth. How is Matt Engarde?

Japanese: 連行は、狩魔検事に まかせてある。
English: I have left Ms. von Karma in charge of his incarceration.

Japanese: 今ごろ、ムチのフルコースを 食らっていることだろう。
English: I'm sure he's getting a full- course meal of whip leather right about now.

Japanese: ‥‥けっこうです。
English: Very good.

Japanese: あぶないところでしたね ‥‥証人。
English: That was a close one, wasn't it, witness?

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。
English: Yes...

Japanese: 私も、自分の罪は つぐなうつもりです。
English: I plan to pay my debt to society for my own crime, Your Honor.

Japanese: 今度の事件で、はじめて 証言台に立たされたとき‥‥
English: This trial was the first time I had stood on the witness stand, and when I did...

Japanese: 私、本当に心細かったんです。
English: I really felt hopeless.

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥）
English: (She must be talking about the time Edgeworth really went after her...)

Japanese: （あのときの御剣‥‥ ヨウシャなかったからな‥‥）
English: (I guess she's trying to forgive him for what he did...)

Japanese: ‥‥そこの証人は‥‥ ある種、病気と言っていい。
English: This witness... How should I put this... She has an illness.

Japanese: 証人が“死”を選ぼうと、 私の知ったことではない‥‥。
English: If you're going to say you would "choose death", that is of no concern to me.

Japanese: でも‥‥あのあと、 留置所でひとりきりになって‥‥
English: But... after that, when I was alone at the detention center...

Japanese: 私、はじめて自分と 向き合うことができました。
English: That's the first time I really saw myself and who I am.

Japanese: そして、今日。
English: And today...

Japanese: おふたりのチカラで、 王都楼が有罪になったとき‥‥
English: When the two of you used your combined strength to convict Matt...

Japanese: 私、やっと ‥‥救われたんです。
English: I... I felt like I had finally been saved.

Japanese: （はじめて見たぞ‥‥ 彼女の笑顔‥‥）
English: (Wow, this is the first time I've ever seen her smile.)

Japanese: あなたたちが事件を 担当してくださって‥‥
English: I am really happy that you two were in charge of this case.

Japanese: ‥‥ホントによかった。
English: I really don't know how to express how I feel at this moment...

Japanese: 私‥‥はじめて、 私自身になれたような気がします。
English: This is... This is the first time I've felt comfortable with myself; with who I am.

Japanese: みなさん。どうも、 ありがとうございました！
English: Thank you so much, everyone!

Japanese: ‥‥ついに、すべてに 決着がついたようですな。
English: It looks like we have resolved everything at last.

Japanese: 私自身は、ちょっとコンラン しているところもあるのですが‥‥
English: As for myself, there are still a few things I'm confused about...

Japanese: ま。みなさん、ハレバレと してらっしゃるから、
English: But everyone seems to be in good spirits,

Japanese: これでヨシとしておきましょう。
English: and that is good enough for me.

Japanese: では、今度こそ‥‥ 本日はこれにて閉廷します！
English: That is all. This court is adjourned!

Japanese: 同日　午後　５時１４分 地方裁判所　被告人第３控え室
English: March 23, 5:14 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 3

Japanese: やっぱり、あなたは最高ね！ なるほどくん。
English: You were great out there, Phoenix!

Japanese: これで‥‥よかったんですよね。
English: What I did out there was right... wasn't it?

Japanese: ‥‥依頼人がいない 休憩室は、はじめてね。
English: This is the first time you've not gotten your client off.

Japanese: あなたが手にしたのは、 たしかに“無罪判決”ではないわ。
English: You got them a "guilty" verdict this time.

Japanese: でも‥‥あなたにも わかったでしょう？
English: But... You have to look past all of that, to what's really important.

Japanese: 弁護士にとって、“無罪判決”より 大きなものがあることを。
English: You now realize that there is something more than just getting a "not guilty", right?

Japanese: ‥‥はい！
English: Yes, I understand now.

Japanese: なるほどくん、思い出してみて。
English: Phoenix, think back for a second.

Japanese: 狩魔検事が証拠品を とどけてくれる前に、
English: Think to the moments before Ms. von Karma arrived with the final pieces of evidence.

Japanese: あなたが一度、 大きな決断をしたことを‥‥
English: Think about the incredible decision you had to make...

Japanese: ‥‥では、弁護人。
English: Now then, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: あなたの、最終的な 結論を聞かせてもらいましょう。
English: Let's hear the defense's final statements on this matter.

Japanese: （もう、証拠はアテにならない。 心の声にしたがうんだ！）
English: (I can't count on the evidence to help me anymore. I have to listen to my heart!)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんの命を取るか‥‥ 正義を取るか‥‥？）
English: (Should I side with justice? Or should I save Maya's life?)

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくの依頼人は‥‥ 王都楼　真悟は‥‥
English: My client... Matt Engarde is...

Japanese: ‥‥“有罪”か“無罪”か‥‥？
English: Is he "guilty"... or is he "not guilty"...?

Japanese: あのときの“選択”。 その答えこそが‥‥
English: Those were your choices then. And your answer...

Japanese: あなたにとっての《弁護士》を 象徴していたのよ‥‥
English: Your answer spoke to what being a "lawyer" means to you...

Japanese: 成歩堂！
English: Wright!

Japanese: み‥‥御剣‥‥
English: E-Edgeworth...

Japanese: いいニュースだ。
English: I have good news.

Japanese: 真宵くんが、さきほど ブジ、警察に保護された！
English: Maya is now safe in police custody!

Japanese: ホントですかッ！
English: Really!?

Japanese: は‥‥春美ちゃ
English: P-Pearls--

Japanese: ホントなのでしょうね！ みつるぎ検事さんッ！
English: You're telling us the truth, right Mr. Edgeworth!?

Japanese: む‥‥むむ。 ま、まちがいない。
English: Y-Yes... She's quite safe.

Japanese: ただ今、署のクルマで こちらに向かっている。
English: She is on her way here as we speak in a patrol car.

Japanese: きゃあああああっ！ 真宵さまが！　真宵さまがアアッ！
English: Aaaaaaaaah! Mystic Maya! Mystic Maya's safe!!

Japanese: やりましたね！　やったんですね！ さすが、なるほどくん！
English: You did it! You really did it, Mr. Nick!

Japanese: （な‥‥なんて重いパンチだ‥‥）
English: (Oww... She punches deceptively hard for a kid...)

Japanese: わたくし‥‥わたくし、 信じていました。
English: I... I believed in you.

Japanese: なるほどくんなら‥‥ なるほどくんなら‥‥
English: I kept saying to myself: Mr. Nick will save her... Mr. Nick will save her...

Japanese: うわああああああああああああん
English: Waaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: ああ‥‥ありがとう。
English: Ah, um... Thanks.
Oh...

Japanese: どうしたの？
English: What's wrong?

Japanese: かるま検事さん‥‥
English: Ms. von Karma...

Japanese: さ、さっきはどうも‥‥
English: Um... About earlier... Uh... Thanks...

Japanese: ‥‥いてッ！
English: OWW!!

Japanese: なぜ、笑っている？ ‥‥成歩堂　龍一！
English: Why are you still smiling... Mr. Phoenix Wright!?

Japanese: あなたは‥‥負けたのよ！
English: You... You lost!!

Japanese: 今まで無敗だった経歴が‥‥ ゼロになった！
English: Your perfect win record has now been crushed!

Japanese: なのに、なぜ‥‥ そんなにうれしそうなのッ！
English: And yet... You are still happy!?

Japanese: きみにはわからないだろうな。 ‥‥狩魔検事。
English: I don't think you'll ever understand... Ms. von Karma.

Japanese: なんですって‥‥！
English: How dare you!?

Japanese: ‥‥ムリもない。１年前までは‥‥ 私もそうだったのだから。
English: Don't worry. She may in time. After all, I was like that myself, until a year ago...
E-Edgeworth...?

Japanese: 自らの勝利のために‥‥ 有罪判決のために‥‥
English: For my own personal victories... and for guilty verdicts...

Japanese: 私はあらゆる手段を使い、 そして‥‥勝利しつづけた。
English: I used every dirty trick in the book. And so my win record remained spotless.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし‥‥
English: But...

Japanese: ‥‥そんな私の前に、 ひとりの男が立ちふさがった。
English: A man appeared and stood fast against that selfish me.

Japanese: 私はいつものように戦い、 そして‥‥初めて敗北した。
English: I fought him in my usual manner, and tasted my first defeat.

Japanese: ‥‥すべてを失ったような 気分だった。
English: I felt like I had lost everything because of that.

Japanese: その後‥‥
English: And then...

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！ 敵を‥‥信頼‥‥？
English: Wh-What!? You trusted your enemy!?

Japanese: だれかが、どんなに キタナイ手を使っても‥‥
English: It doesn't matter how many underhanded tricks a person uses...

Japanese: 真実はかならず、カオを出す。
English: The truth will always find a way to make itself known.

Japanese: われわれにできるのは、 全存在をかけて戦うことだけだ。
English: The only thing we can do is to fight with the knowledge we hold and everything we have.

Japanese: ‥‥やがて、ナゾは１つずつ すがたを消して‥‥
English: Erasing the paradoxes one by one...

Japanese: 最後にわれわれは、たどりつく。 ‥‥かならず。
English: It's never easy... We claw and scratch for every inch.

Japanese: ‥‥１つしかない“真相”に。
English: But we will always eventually reach that one single truth. This I promise you.

Japanese: “しんそう”‥‥
English: The "truth"...

Japanese: ‥‥そう。検事と弁護士は、 そのために存在しているのだ。
English: Yes. That's the reason why prosecutors and defense lawyers exist.

Japanese: 成歩堂。もちろん、 キミはそれを知っていた。
English: But I'm sure you knew that already, didn't you Wright?

Japanese: だから‥‥すがたを消した 私を、ユルせなかったのだろう。
English: That's why you couldn't forgive me; this man who went into hiding. Isn't that right?

Japanese: 自分の勝利のみしか目に入らずに すがたを消した、この私を‥‥
English: This man who only had his sights set on "victory", who ran away into the night...
Ah!

Japanese: そ、そうだったのですか！ なるほどくん‥‥！
English: Is... Is Mr. Edgeworth right, Mr. Nick...!?

Japanese: （‥‥ウラ切られた‥‥）
English: (You really let me down...)

Japanese: お前の失踪を知ったとき、 そう感じた‥‥
English: When you disappeared, I felt... betrayed.

Japanese: ぼくが弁護士になる決心を したのは‥‥
English: The reason I decided to become a lawyer to begin with...

Japanese: ずっと昔‥‥お前が語った言葉を 信じていたからなのに。
English: Was because I believed in the things you said to me, all those years ago...

Japanese: その言葉を‥‥ お前自身がウラ切った。
English: And you... You betrayed your own words.

Japanese: ‥‥だから‥‥１年前。 ぼくは心に決めた。
English: That's why... one year ago, I made up my mind.

Japanese: ぼくの知っていた御剣　怜侍は もう、死んだんだ‥‥
English: I decided that the Miles Edgeworth I knew had died...

Japanese: ‥‥そう思おう、って‥‥。
English: ...At least, that's what I told myself.

Japanese: くだらないッ！
English: You pathetic fool!

Japanese: か‥‥かるま検事さん‥‥
English: M-Ms. von Karma...

Japanese: 敗者の逃げ口上など 聞きたくないわ！
English: I don't want to hear the wretched whimpering of a disgraced loser!

Japanese: 狩魔は‥‥カンペキである 宿命を背負っているのよ！
English: A Von Karma is someone who is destined to be perfect!

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍‥‥ 見そこなったわ！
English: Miles Edgeworth... You are no longer worthy!

Japanese: キサマはもう、狩魔の 人間ではない！
English: You are no longer worthy of being a Von Karma!

Japanese: ‥‥そして、この私も！
English: And neither am I!

Japanese: もう‥‥すべては 終わったのよ！
English: It's over... It's all over!

Japanese: （‥‥狩魔のヤツ、 何か投げつけていったぞ‥‥）
English: (Franziska threw something on the ground just now...)

Japanese: これは‥‥電波受信機だ。
English: This is... an electromagnetic receiver.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事に セットしてあったヤツか‥‥
English: Isn't that the thing she used to track Detective Gumshoe...?

Japanese: ‥‥あとで、私のほうから 署のほうに返しておこう。
English: I'll return this to the precinct later.

Japanese: そして‥‥もうひとつは‥‥
English: There's something else...

Japanese: あ。かるま検事さんの ムチではないですか！
English: Ah! Isn't that Ms. von Karma's whip!?

Japanese: ‥‥“もう、法廷に 立つことはない”‥‥
English: "I'll never set foot in another courtroom again"...

Japanese: そういうコトなのだろうな。
English: I'm sure that's what she's saying by this action...

Japanese: ‥‥これは、キミが 受けとるべきだな、成歩堂。
English: You should keep this, Wright.

Japanese: ああ。そうするよ。
English: Umm... OK.

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくんッ！
English: Nick!!

Japanese: ‥‥真宵ちゃんッ！
English: MAYA!

Japanese: 真宵さま‥‥！
English: Mystic Maya!

Japanese: マヨイさまああああっ！
English: Mystic Mayaaaaaaaa!

Japanese: いやあ、さっすが なるほどくんだよねえ！
English: Oh, Nick! I knew you would come through!

Japanese: ヤクソクどおり、アイツを 有罪にして‥‥
English: You got Engarde convicted, like I knew you would...

Japanese: そのうえ、このあたしまで 助けてくれちゃってさ！
English: And on top of that, you even rescued me!

Japanese: なんで、あんなムチャな ヤクソクをさせたんだよ！
English: Well, of course I did! You know I would never desert you!

Japanese: もう少しで‥‥ 死んじゃうところだったんだぞ！
English: But we sure pressed our luck this trial... You're really lucky to be standing here!

Japanese: いいのいいの。 ‥‥そのときはホラ！
English: Whatever, whatever. Look, it's over, OK? Besides, if I did croak,

Japanese: はみちゃんに乗りうつって、 バケて出ればすむハナシでしょ！
English: I would just come back and haunt you like a bad ghost through Pearly!

Japanese: （そんなカンタンな ハナシだったのか‥‥？）
English: (Is it really that easy to do something like that...?)

Japanese: どうも、ありがとう。 ‥‥なるほどくん。
English: Th-Thanks a lot... Nick.
Um... Don't mention it.

Japanese: 真宵くん‥‥。
English: Maya...

Japanese: あ。御剣検事。
English: Oh, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: その‥‥。おめでとう。
English: Um... I'm relieved you're alright.

Japanese: おっ。さすが御剣検事。 ちょっと進歩しましたね！
English: Hey... It looks like you've made some real progress, Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: うむう‥‥
English: Umm...

Japanese: まあ、１年前の私とは ちょっとちがうかもしれない。
English: Well, I suppose I'm a little different from who I was a year ago.

Japanese: へええ‥‥
English: Heh...

Japanese: ‥ぐぎゅるるぐぐ‥‥ぅぅ‥‥う‥
English: *growwwwl...grrrrr...urrrr...*

Japanese: ‥‥よし！　じゃ、 みんなで行こうか！
English: Alright! I think it's time we got out of this depressing place!

Japanese: え‥‥ど、どこに？
English: Huh? Where are we going!?

Japanese: 決まってるでしょ！ ゴハンだよ、ゴ・ハ・ン！
English: Food, Nick! FOOD! Grub! Chow! I'm starved!

Japanese: あたしもう、なるほどくんが フライドチキンに見えるんだから！
English: I'm so hungry even you look like a nice, juicy burger on a bun to me, Nick!

Japanese: フライドチキン‥‥
English: Y-You think I look like a burger...? I'm a Prime Rib at least!

Japanese: ‥‥あ！　みつるぎ検事さんも ごいっしょに、どうぞ！
English: Come with us, Mr. Edgeworth! Please!!

Japanese: う、うむ。では、 おコトバにあまえて‥‥
English: Uh, um... If you insist...

Japanese: ‥‥そうだな。じゃあ、 いつものラーメン屋に‥‥
English: Alright... So how about we hit up our usual burger joint...?

Japanese: ナニ言ってるの、なるほどくん。
English: Don't be silly, Nick.
Huh?

Japanese: 今度の事件で、あたしの舌、 すっかり肥えちゃったからね。
English: This case messed up that awesome evening, and got in the way of my gourmet food.

Japanese: もう一度、ディナーの やりなおしに決まってるでしょ！
English: So I've decided that we have to make it up by having another feast!

Japanese: ‥‥でぃなー‥‥
English: A-Another feast...?

Japanese: さあ！　食べるぞお！
English: C'mon, Nick! FOOOOOOD!

Japanese: 同日　午後　７時３８分 ホテル・バンドー　ロビー
English: March 23, 7:38 PM Gatewater Hotel Hotel Lobby

Japanese: おそいッス！ 待ちくたびれたッス！
English: Hey, pal! Sorry to keep you guys waiting!

Japanese: イトノコさん！ だいじょうぶなんですか！
English: Gumshoe! Are you alright!?

Japanese: いやー、メンボクないッス。
English: Yeah, but I'm really embarrassed.

Japanese: まさか、電柱にぶつかるとは 思わなかったッス。
English: I didn't think I would hit a telephone pole, of all things.

Japanese: でんちゅう‥‥ （赤信号は関係なかったのか‥‥）
English: A telephone pole...? (Then, it wasn't a red light that got him...?)

Japanese: やったなァ、ナルホドー。
English: Ya did it again, City Boy.

Japanese: また今回も、シンゾーにワルい、 イヤーな感じやったで！
English: I felt like my dear ol' heart was gonna give out on me! And I ain't jokin'!

Japanese: トノサマンの最終回よりも ドキドキしましたよ！
English: Yeah! It was more exciting than the very last episode of the Steel Samurai!

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥恐縮です。
English: Th-Thanks...

Japanese: コラ、検事！　アンタちょっと イジメすぎやで、ナルホドーを。
English: Now lookie here, mister snooty prosecutor! Don't ya reckon ya bullied Mr. Wright too hard!?

Japanese: もっと、いたわってやらんと。 いーかげん、死ぬで。今夜あたり。
English: If ya don't start bein' a lot nicer to him, he might just kick it... Tonight, even!

Japanese: ム‥‥ ココロがけよう。
English: Umm... I'll keep that in mind.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、みんなァ！ ならんでならんで！
English: Well, come on now! Everyone gather 'round!

Japanese: プロのカメラマンが、 記念写真、とったるでー。
English: Y'all are gonna get yer picture taken by a genuine professional photographer!

Japanese: （ナツミさん、新しい カメラ、買ったんだ‥‥）
English: (Looks like Lotta bought herself a new camera...)

Japanese: ‥‥ま。ま。とにかく、 事件は終わったッス。
English: Well, pal... At least we can put this messy case behind us now.

Japanese: さあ！　今夜はバンバン 食べるッス！
English: Come on! Tonight's all about eating, so let's go chow down, pal!

Japanese: 食べるッス！
English: Amen to that, pal! Amen!

Japanese: それにしても、 お手がらでしたね、刑事さん！
English: You know, when you think about it, you were the one who saved the day, Detective!

Japanese: え？　え？ ‥‥そ、そッスか？
English: Huh? Me? ...You really think so?

Japanese: そうだな‥‥
English: He's right.

Japanese: キサマが持ち出した ３つの遺留品がなければ‥‥
English: If it wasn't for the three items you took...

Japanese: おそらく、こういう結果には ならなかっただろう。
English: I think this trial would have had a very different ending.

Japanese: いや。まあ。なんだ。その。 はは。はははは。ははははは。
English: Ah, well, you know... It's... Ho ho. Ho ho ho ho. Ho ho ho ho ho.

Japanese: ‥‥アレ？
English: ...Huh? Wait.

Japanese: ‥‥おかしいッスね‥‥。
English: That's odd...

Japanese: 自分が現場から 持ち出した遺留品は‥‥
English: When I ran off with the things from De Killer's hideout...

Japanese: ゼンブで‥‥ ４つ、あったはずッス。
English: I was sure I took 4 things total, sir.
What?

Japanese: ４つ‥‥ですか‥‥？
English: Four?

Japanese: たしか、コートのポッケに 入れておいたッスが‥‥
English: Yeah, I'm sure I put one of the items in my coat pocket...

Japanese: （４つ目の遺留品‥‥か）
English: (There was a fourth item...?)

Japanese: ま。ま。ええやんか。 もう、事件は終わったんや！
English: Aw, come on y'all. It's over!

Japanese: そんなコトより、 アンタもアレやなー。
English: But whoo boy, I tell ya! You really are somethin' else!

Japanese: 殺人犯にされてみたり、 ユーカイされてみたり‥‥
English: Between getting' accused of murder and getting' kidnapped...

Japanese: タイクツしないわなー、 見ていて。
English: Never a dull moment with you, huh?

Japanese: えへへ。‥‥そうですか？
English: Hahaha, you think?

Japanese: （なんで うれしそうなんだ‥‥？）
English: (Why does she look so happy about that...?)

Japanese: ‥‥でも、まるまる２日間も 閉じこめられてしまって‥‥
English: But being shut away for two whole days...

Japanese: さぞ、コワかったのでは‥‥？
English: Weren't you scared...?

Japanese: そうだねー。 やっぱりね、心細かったなあ。
English: Yeah, it was really scary. I felt so hopeless.

Japanese: 気をまぎらわそうと思ってさ。 絵、かいてたんだよ！
English: So to keep my mind off of things, I drew a picture!

Japanese: のんきなヤッちゃなー。 お絵かきするかァ？　フツー。
English: Sounds like ya had it rough, gal! So where's this picture of yers?

Japanese: わあ！　見たいです、 真宵さまの絵‥‥
English: Yeah! I want to see it! I want to see Mystic Maya's picture!

Japanese: そういえば、どこか 行っちゃったな、あの絵。
English: Hmm... You know, I don't know where it went...

Japanese: ううう‥‥ ちょっぴりザンネンです‥‥
English: Aww... That's too bad...

Japanese: ま‥‥まあ、いいじゃない！ たいしたモンじゃないし。
English: W-Well, it's alright! It wasn't anything important anyway.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥） (Ah...)
（やっと‥‥ふたりに 笑顔がもどったな‥‥）
English: (It sure is nice to finally see them both smiling again.)

Japanese: ‥ピピッ‥‥ピピッ‥‥ピピッ‥
English: ...*beep* *beep*... ...*beep* *beep*... ...*beep* *beep*...

Japanese: どうした？　御剣。
English: What is it, Edgeworth?

Japanese: コイツが、動き出した。
English: This thing is picking something up.

Japanese: あ！　それ！ 狩魔検事の電波受信機！
English: Ah! That's! That's Ms. von Karma's receiver!

Japanese: ッたく、あのヒトのおかげで ヒドい目にあったッス！
English: Ugh. Thanks to her, I had the most awful experience of my life, sir!

Japanese: この自分に、発信器を つけるなんて‥‥
English: I can't believe she stuck a tracking device on me...

Japanese: ‥‥妙だな。
English: That's odd.

Japanese: イトノコギリ刑事。 キサマはここにいるのに‥‥
English: Even though you're standing right here...

Japanese: 発信器は‥‥まるで ベツの場所で反応している。
English: The tracking device seems to be in a different location.

Japanese: はああ‥‥ コショウしたッスかねえ‥‥
English: Oh... It's probably busted or something, sir.

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、いい。
English: ...Well, it doesn't matter.

Japanese: さて。私はそろそろ シツレイしよう。
English: I'm afraid it's about time for me to excuse myself.

Japanese: まだ、シゴトも 残っているからな。
English: I still have some work to do.

Japanese: えー！　検事さん、まだ ぜんぜん食べてないのに。
English: Huh!? But Mr. Edgeworth! You haven't even eaten anything yet!

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんが 食べすぎなんだよ！）
English: (And you've eaten way too much, you glutton!)

Japanese: ‥‥今夜は楽しかった。 では‥‥シツレイする。
English: I had fun tonight. Now, if you'll excuse me...

Japanese: 待ってくれ。
English: Wait.

Japanese: なんだ？
English: What?

Japanese: 今回は、ありがとう。 ‥‥礼を言うよ。
English: I just want to say... Thanks, Edgeworth. You really saved me out there.

Japanese: ‥‥フッ。
English: ...Hmph.

Japanese: 礼を言うのは‥‥ 私のほうだ、成歩堂。
English: If anyone should be saying thanks, it should be me, Wright.

Japanese: （コトバだけじゃ少し、 足りない気がするな‥‥）
English: (I feel like words alone aren't enough here...)

Japanese: （何か、わたしておくべき ものは、ないかな‥‥？）
English: (I wonder if there's anything I can give him to express how I feel...?)

Japanese: なんだ、これは。
English: What's this...?

Japanese: え‥‥。 お礼のつもり、だけど‥‥
English: Um... It's my way of saying thanks...

Japanese: そうか、わかった。 ‥‥気持ちだけ受けとっておこう。
English: I... see... Well, it's the thought that counts...

Japanese: （うーん‥‥どうも、 伝わらなかったみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... I guess I'm not very good at this showing my appreciation thing...)
What's this...?

Japanese: ありがとう。 ‥‥お前たちのおかげだよ。
English: Thank you... It's all thanks to you two.

Japanese: お前と‥‥彼女の。
English: You... and her...

Japanese: ‥‥礼にはおよばない。 私は、自分のシゴトをしただけだ。
English: You don't need to thank me. I was only doing my job.

Japanese: ‥‥行っちゃいましたね、 みつるぎ検事さん。
English: It looks like Mr. Edgeworth has left, Mr. Nick.

Japanese: ねえ。真宵さま。
English: Hey, Mystic Maya?

Japanese: ん？　なあに？
English: Hm? Yes, Pearly?

Japanese: これで、また ホレなおしてしまいましたね。
English: I guess you two can go back to being lovey-dovey, right?

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくんに。
English: You and Mr. Nick, I mean...

Japanese: も‥‥もう！ やめてよ！　はみちゃん！
English: P-Pearly! Would you cut it out already!? You're embarrassing me!

Japanese: ところで‥‥
English: Um, anyway...

Japanese: こんなリッパなディナー‥‥ いったい、どなたが？
English: So, who's paying for this lovely dinner party?

Japanese: 決まってるじゃないですか。
English: As if you need to ask!

Japanese: ごちそうさま、なるほどくん！
English: Everyone say, "Thank you" to Nick!

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: いやー、なにぶん自分は ソーメンも食えない身分ッスから。
English: Ah, yeah... I'm kinda at the point where I can't even buy instant noodles, pal.

Japanese: とっさに、アンタの名前で 予約を入れちまったッス。
English: So I kinda already put your name on the bill.

Japanese: え。え。
English: Huh? Huh?

Japanese: いやー、ウチもなァ。
English: Yeah, I got me a situation just like that myself.

Japanese: ホテルの中にカメラ屋が あったからなァ、
English: There's this camera shop in this hotel, see...

Japanese: 買っちゃったワ、コレ。 ３０万円の最高級品。
English: And I just bought myself this good ol' beauty here! It'd better be anyhow for $3,000!

Japanese: え。え。え。
English: Huh? Huh? Huh?

Japanese: とっさに、アンタの名前で 書いといたからナ。サイン。
English: Actually, I reckon ya bought it for me since it's on yer tab and all.

Japanese: え。え。え。え。
English: Huh? Huh? Huh? Huh?

Japanese: よかったですね！　なるほどくん。
English: Isn't this great, Mr. Nick!?

Japanese: いやー、ホントにね。
English: Yeah, Nick!

Japanese: （ここで‥‥やっぱりぼくは 叫んでおくべきなのか‥‥？）
English: (Why do I suddenly feel like screaming?)

Japanese: エンリョいらんで。
English: Aww, ya don't need to hold back now, ya hear?

Japanese: チカラいっぱい、 行っとくッス！
English: Yeah, pal! Time to let it all out!

Japanese: ‥‥わたくし、ナマで聞くの、 はじめてかもしれません。
English: This is going to be the first time I hear the real you.

Japanese: あたしもひさしぶりだな。 ユーカイされてたから。
English: Go on! It's been a while since I heard you say it. I've been busy being a hostage and all.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥ おコトバにあまえて。
English: Alright then... If you say so.

Japanese: ■企画・脚本・監督 巧　　舟
English: ■Planning/Script/Director Shuu Takumi

Japanese: やっぱり、なるほどくんは たよりになるよねえ！
English: You really came through for me, Nick!

Japanese: 手紙じゃあ、ちょっとカッコつけ ちゃったけど、信じてたもん。
English: I had to hide that letter, but I knew you'd find it.

Japanese: ‥‥それにしても、ホント最近、 ついてないよなあ。
English: I really feel like I've been living on the edge lately.

Japanese: フツーなら、もう３回ぐらい 死んでるもん。すごいよ、あたし。
English: I mean, I've escaped death three times now! Pretty cool, huh? I feel like a pro!

Japanese: ■デザイン・原画 岩元　辰郎
English: ■Design/Art Tatsurou Iwamoto

Japanese: なるほどくんが真宵さまを 助けてくれて‥‥
English: I'm so happy that you could save Mystic Maya, Mr. Nick!

Japanese: わたくし、もう うれしくてうれしくて！
English: And I'm so happy for the two of you!

Japanese: そういえば‥‥このホテルには ユメのような結婚式場があるとか。
English: Speaking of... I think this hotel is a popular place for honeymooners...

Japanese: おふたりのために、ちょっと わたくしが、ご予約を‥‥。
English: So I sort of... made reservations for the two of you... Just in case...

Japanese: ■背景グラフィック 村本　シュウイチ 山本　晃一朗
English: ■Background Graphics Shuuichi Muramoto Kouichirou Yamamoto

Japanese: どうやら、自分も刑事に もどるコトができそうッス。
English: Well, pal. It looks like I'm back on the force again.

Japanese: 御剣検事が、自分のために 署長にかけあってくれたッス！
English: Mr. Edgeworth had a long talk with the Chief, and he got me reinstated for my sake!

Japanese: “アイツを野放しにしてはマズい” ‥‥ってコトになったらしいッス。
English: I heard he said things like, "Letting that one go is bad for all of society."

Japanese: オトコはやっぱり、ガツンと ぶつかって行くべきッスね！
English: I knew it! Crashing headlong into everything is the only way to live, pal!

Japanese: ■人物グラフィック 武田　清子 遠藤　繁雄
English: ■Character Graphics Seiko Takeda Shigeo Endou

Japanese: スズキ　マコ、今日でこの 制服を脱ぐッス！
English: I, Maggey Byrde, am retiring this uniform as of today, sir!

Japanese: これからは、レストランの ウエイトレスさんとして、
English: I'm going to be a waitress from now on.

Japanese: みんなにエガオとシアワセと メシを運ぶッス！
English: And bring smiles and joy to the people who come by the restaurant, sir!

Japanese: 成歩堂さん‥‥いつかまた、 お会いしましょうねッ！
English: I hope you'll stop by sometime, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ■グラフィック補佐 斉藤　昌志 中野　幸司
English: ■Graphics Assistance Masashi Saitou Kouji Nakano

Japanese: ん。ご面会かな？　ん。 院長の堀田ですわ。ほっほっ。
English: Hmm, yes... Are you here to visit a patient? Hmm... I'm Director Hotti... Hoh, hoh.

Japanese: 最近ね。ん。ホレ。あのコ、 見かけないんだよねコレが。ん。
English: Recently... Hmm, yes... That girl, you know... I haven't seen her around... Hmm, yes...

Japanese: 一目ボレ、っていうのかな。ん。 なんかこう、パチッと来たワケよ。
English: But I remember... If I even laid so much as an eye on her, it would go, "Crack!". Hmm...

Japanese: ‥‥ま。ムチでたたかれたから かもしれんけど。ん。ん。
English: ...It didn't matter if I got whipped though... Hmm... Hmm, yes... Hoh, hoh.

Japanese: ■メインプログラム 山本　忠晃
English: ■Main Programming Tadaaki Yamamoto

Japanese: ついに、ボクたちの 世界征服が始まるよハニイ！
English: It's time to begin our quest of world circus domination sweetie!!

Japanese: 手始めにね。アメリカへ 飛ぶんだ。この夏に！
English: And to let the world know we are serious, I plan to make a fabulous flight to Zimbabwe!

Japanese: ねえ、マックス！　どんなところ かなあ、アメリカって。
English: Hey Max! What do you think Zimbabwe is like?

Japanese: ケーキでできたお城とか、 お話しするウサギさんとか‥‥
English: Do you think there are castles made of cake, and bunnies who can talk...?

Japanese: 少なくとも、トミーのギャグで笑う ウサギくんはいないと思うよ！
English: I think if there are any talking bunnies, even they won't laugh at Moe's jokes!

Japanese: ■プログラム 弥冨　英樹 園山　一輝
English: ■Programming Hideki Yatomi Kazuki Sonoyama

Japanese: オレっちも、ネタの ジュンビでおおいそがしさ！
English: I'm ready! I'm ready! There's no way these jokes are gonna fall on deaf ears.

Japanese: ジャパニーズ・スマイルとは、 今度こそお別れだぜ！
English: I'm going to be more contemporary with my humor! Moe Curls, R-R-R-Represent!

Japanese: オレたちの新ネタも決まったぜ！ “第九”を大合唱してやるんだ！
English: We've got our new act all worked out! Prepare for the "Hallelujah Chorus"!

Japanese: なんとか言えよなー。 やるのはオマエなんだからさー。
English: Say something will you! You're supposed to start this off! Get on with it...!!

Japanese: ■音楽・効果音 木村　明美
English: ■Music/Sound Effects Akemi Kimura

Japanese: ‥‥なんだい！　充電中の ヒゲソリじゃないか！
English: ...What's this!? Drat, it's just an ordinary electric razor recharging on its stand!

Japanese: マッタク、いつまで こんなコトさせるつもりなんだい！
English: I can't believe this. Really! How long do they plan on making me do this!?

Japanese: ま。ミッちゃんのコトだ、ナニか 深ぁい考えがあるんだろうケド。
English: Ah, but it's Edgey-poo's idea, so that means it must have a deep, hidden meaning.

Japanese: まさか‥‥忘れてる‥‥ なんてコト、ないよネェ。
English: But... Why do I get the feeling... They wouldn't forget about me, would they?

Japanese: ムカシはこうじゃなかったョ！ みいんな、オバチャンを女王サマ
English: Ah, it was never like this in the old days! Everyone thought the world of me! They used to

Japanese: みたいにチヤホヤしちゃってさァ、 言うコト聞かないオトコなんかもお
English: call me Queen Wendy, and treat me like royalty, and any man who hasn't heard about

Japanese: カカトでメリメリふみつけてやった っちゅうハナシだョそれになんだ
English: this is going to feel the pain of my heel, yes they're going to feel the burn, and speaking

Japanese: アブナイ火アソビだってたしなんで るワケョおかげで大道具の倉庫３つ
English: of burn, playing with fire is very dangerous and because of that, three of the warehouses

Japanese: もやしちゃってこれがバレちゃって そりゃポカスカに殴られた殴られた
English: where they stored the scenery were burned right down and that caused a huge stink that

Japanese: ■制作協力 丸永　克己 工藤　寛之
English: ■Special Thanks Katsumi Marunaga Hiroyuki Kudou

Japanese: ■制作協力 末包　久美子 諏訪部　ゆかり
English: ■Special Thanks Kumiko Suekane Yukari Suwabe

Japanese: 成歩堂さんと御剣さんには、 本当にカンシャしています。
English: I appreciate everything you and Mr. Edgeworth did for me from the bottom of my heart.

Japanese: あ。そういえば、狩魔検事さんから お手紙がとどいたんですよ。
English: Oh, that's right. I received a letter from Ms. von Karma.

Japanese: 私が出所したら、なんでも 相談に乗らせてほしい、って。
English: She said that after I get out, I should feel free to consult her about anything at all.

Japanese: ‥‥みなさんに出会えて、 ホントによかった‥‥！
English: I'm really thankful to have met everyone!

Japanese: ■プロデュース 稲葉　敦志
English: ■Producer Atsushi Inaba

Japanese: ‥‥この国にも‥‥ いづらくなってまいりました。
English: It has become difficult for me in this country as of late.

Japanese: しばらくは、身をかくす コトになりそうですので‥‥
English: As such, I will take a short leave of absence.

Japanese: ご依頼のほうは、ホームページの ほうからおねがいします。
English: If you would like to request my services, please be sure to visit my homepage.

Japanese: では‥‥おたがい、長生き したいものでございますね。
English: May we both be blessed with longevity.

Japanese: ■製作総指揮 三上　真司
English: ■Executive Producer Shinji Mikami

Japanese: 同日　午後　９時４２分 国際エアライン　１２番搭乗口
English: March 23, 9:42 PM International Departures Gate 12

Japanese: ‥‥どこへ行く？　メイ‥‥
English: Where are you going... Franziska?

Japanese: ど、どうしてここが‥‥
English: How did you know I was here...?

Japanese: ‥‥コイツだ。
English: With this.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥
English: That's...

Japanese: キミは、あの男に発信器を 取りつけていたそうだな。
English: I heard you were planting things on a certain person.

Japanese: ‥‥おそらく、 彼の‥‥コートに。
English: Things like tracking devices in his coat, for example.

Japanese: フッ‥‥さすがね。
English: Hmph. That's just like you.

Japanese: あの刑事‥‥ いつも着ていたから。
English: I only planted it there because he was always wearing it.

Japanese: この、 うすぎたないコートを‥‥。
English: This... filthy, drab coat of his...

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、ニモツに まぎれこんでいたみたい。
English: I don't know how it ended up in my luggage.

Japanese: あとで、ゴミ箱へ 捨てておくわ。
English: But it's going in the trash, I promise you that.

Japanese: ‥‥ああ、そうだ。 コートといえば‥‥
English: Oh, that's right... Speaking of that man...

Japanese: あの男が、こんなコトを 言っていたのだが‥‥
English: He told me something very interesting.

Japanese: 自分が現場から 持ち出した遺留品は‥‥
English: When I ran off with the things from De Killer's hideout...

Japanese: ゼンブで‥‥ ４つ、あったはずッス。
English: I was sure I took 4 things total, sir.

Japanese: ‥‥４つ目の遺留品‥‥？
English: Four items...?

Japanese: コートのポケットに 入れておいたらしいが。
English: It seems he put the last one in his coat pocket.

Japanese: このコートの‥‥？
English: He put it in here...?

Japanese: 事件はもう、解決した。 もう‥‥、どうでもいいわ。
English: It doesn't matter anymore. The case is already over.

Japanese: これから、どうするつもりだ？
English: What are you going to do now?

Japanese: あなたには関係ないわ。
English: That's none of your business.

Japanese: ‥‥逃げるのか？
English: Are you running away?

Japanese: うるさいッ！
English: Shut up!

Japanese: あなたなんかに わかるはずがないわね‥‥。
English: You don't understand a thing!

Japanese: “狩魔　豪の娘”という立場が いったい、どんなものか！
English: You can't possibly understand what it means to be "Manfred von Karma's daughter"!
Franziska...

Japanese: まわりから期待され‥‥ 応えつづけなければならない！
English: So many expectations from everyone around me... Expectations I must fulfill!

Japanese: 勝訴してアタリマエ。 ‥‥負けなんて、考えられない。
English: I'm expected to win no matter what. And failure? Such a thing is not an option for me!

Japanese: 父はたしかに、天才だったわ！ でも‥‥
English: My father was a genius. There's no doubt about that! But...

Japanese: 私はそうじゃない。 ‥‥そんなことは知っていた。
English: But me... I'm no genius. I've always known that.

Japanese: 私は‥‥天才でなければ ならなかったのよ‥‥
English: But I... I had to be one. I had to.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥キミは 天才ではないかもしれない。
English: You may not be a genius like your father...

Japanese: しかし‥‥
English: But...

Japanese: キミは、検事だ。 これまでも‥‥これからも。
English: You are a prosecutor. You have been and always will be.

Japanese: 私には‥‥もう、ムリよ。
English: No, I'm not... Not anymore.

Japanese: だって‥‥もう ムチも捨ててしまったわ。
English: I've even thrown my whip away.

Japanese: そういえば‥‥
English: Speaking of that...

Japanese: 成歩堂から コイツをあずかっている。
English: Wright gave me this to hold onto.
(Wright...)

Japanese: （キサマ、こうなることを 予測していたのか‥‥？）
English: (You knew something like this would happen, didn't you...?)

Japanese: もう一度、言う。
English: I'm going to say this again.

Japanese: われわれは、検事としての 名誉のために戦うのではない。
English: We prosecutors do not fight for personal honor or pride.

Japanese: そのムチが、 何を打つべきか‥‥
English: I hope you will think deeply...

Japanese: よく、考えてみるんだな。
English: About what you should be striking down with that whip.

Japanese: （しまった‥‥）
English: (Argh... Wright...)

Japanese: （成歩堂から、ムチを 受けとってくるべきだったか！）
English: (You were supposed to give me the whip!)

Japanese: あなたは、いつもそうだった‥‥
English: You haven't changed a bit...

Japanese: いつも‥‥私を置いて、 先へ歩いていってしまう。
English: You've always... You've always left me alone and walked on ahead without me.

Japanese: ‥‥御剣　怜侍‥‥ あなたがニクかったわ。
English: Miles Edgeworth... I've always hated you.

Japanese: でも、ついに‥‥あなたに 復讐するチャンスがめぐってきた。
English: And then... Finally, my chance to take my revenge on you arrived.

Japanese: あの男に勝てば‥‥ 成歩堂　龍一を打ち負かせば‥‥
English: If I could win against that man... If I could make Phoenix Wright bow down in defeat...

Japanese: 私を置いていったあなたを 超えることができる！
English: Then this "girl" you left behind would have risen higher than you!

Japanese: ‥‥それが、 私の“復讐”だったの‥‥
English: That was supposed to be my "revenge"...

Japanese: ‥‥そうだったのか‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: ‥‥私にはムリよ‥‥
English: You know, I can't do it...

Japanese: 今までの自分を‥‥ 捨ててしまうなんて。
English: I can't change who I am. I can't throw away everything I've been until today.

Japanese: ‥‥できるさ。 あの華宮　霧緒のように、な。
English: I believe you can. Just like how Adrian Andrews did.

Japanese: ‥‥カミヤ　キリオ‥‥？
English: Adrian Andrews...?

Japanese: キミは、彼女と取り引きして、 利用したつもりだろう。
English: You were going to use her during the trial, right?

Japanese: しかしキミだって、父親に‥‥
English: But you...

Japanese: 狩魔　豪に、 依存していただけではないか。
English: You were "dependant" on your father by using his tactics. Isn't that right?
Hmph!

Japanese: ‥‥キミは今日、 私に追いついたのだ。
English: Today, you chased after me, after I had left you behind all these years.

Japanese: われわれは今、ここに 並んで立っている。
English: And that's why we're standing here now, side by side.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし。 私は立ち止まるつもりはない。
English: But I have no intention of stopping.

Japanese: ‥‥キミが歩くのを やめると言うのならば‥‥
English: If you say you are going to quit your walk down the prosecutor's path...

Japanese: ‥‥ここでお別れだな。 狩魔　冥。
English: Then, this is where we part ways, Franziska von Karma.

Japanese: わ、私は‥‥ 私は狩魔　冥よ。 I... I...
English: I am Franziska von Karma.

Japanese: ‥‥いつまでも、私の前を 歩いていられると思わないで。
English: Don't think I'm going to walk in your shadow forever...

Japanese: 勝負は‥‥これから。 ‥‥カクゴしておきなさい！
English: Our battle... begins now... so you had better prepare yourself, Miles Edgeworth!

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂　龍一‥‥
English: Phoenix Wright...

Japanese: いつか、きっと‥‥
English: One day... Someday...

Japanese: また、法廷で会いましょう。
English: I'm sure we'll meet again in battle.

Japanese: ‥‥その日までは‥‥
English: Until then...

Japanese: あなたにとどけることが できなかった、最後の証拠品‥‥
English: This last piece of evidence that never made it to you...

Japanese: この、４つ目の遺留品は ‥‥私があずかっておくわ‥‥
English: I'll take good care of this fourth piece...

Japanese: あなたに会う、その日まで‥‥
English: So I can give it to you... when at last we meet again...

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、王都楼　真悟に この場で、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Matt Engarde...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. This court is adjourned!
